In Defense of Slimes.name: 黏液怪防御战 In Defense of Slimes
In Defense of Slimes.text:
摘自艾瑟瑞亚·尼克斯的档案
为黏液怪辩护

与那些想用稻草人论证来嘲笑我观点的人所相信的相反，我并不认为无生命物体或真正无心智的存在应当被纳入关于“人”和“怪物”的讨论中。尽管在涉及vitae——所谓的自然灵时——确实存在一些复杂性，它们甚至可以寄寓于灌木或岩石之中，并允许施法者与其交谈；但即便如此，把这些vitae视为世界生命力彼此独立却又相互连接的表达，会更容易理解。至于完全无心智的生物，它们通常连那种程度都没有。对不了解的人来说，甚至可能会惊讶：没有心智也能活着并行动。

但生命总能在一切可能性中找到巧妙的道路。对于这类缺乏集中式心智的存在而言，生物身体的每一部分都会为了其被条件化的活动而各司其职，作为大脑或心智的复合替代品。绝大多数软泥怪正是如此，而也正是这种精确的分布，让人难以对软泥怪的要害发动打击。若其体内每一盎司的黏胶都能执行与其余部分相同的身体机能，那么重要的便是数量而非质量。这是一种很有趣的对抗伤害的防御机制，但它终究会排除心智的发展，除非是在少数罕见情形下。然而，有些软泥怪会发展出某种“核心”——在缺乏更好词汇的情况下——即体内一个比其他部分更强的内核。我们将这些拥有核心的软泥怪称为“黏液怪”。黏液怪能表现出比其他软泥怪更复杂的行为与特殊能力，这取决于它们的核心在黏液怪生理结构中发展来填补何种角色。尽管如此，它们大多数仍然无心智……但有些黏液怪核心会发展成相当于大脑的结构，赋予黏液怪一个有意识的知性心智。起初，这些并不像那些核心提供更直接适用、更壮观或更具魔法性质能力的黏液怪那般强大，但它们最终会随着时间变得强大得多，因为它们能够学习。它们能够成长，而它们的核心也能获得更多特性。

尽管如此，它们看起来就和无心智生物一模一样，而大多数冒险者或旅人都不够开明，无法分辨软泥怪、缺乏智性的黏液怪，以及有智性黏液怪之间的区别。就像许多其他事物一样，黏液怪被迫发展出一种方式，以方便我们弥合这道鸿沟。因此，有智性黏液怪会去学习如何发出我们的语言，利用黏胶粗略仿造我们的形体，甚至试图使用那些大多数黏液怪通常并不需要的外在装饰，比如衣物或性别。这并不是说黏液怪从不会自行渴望这些事物——我至少知道一个案例：黏液怪的核心觉醒出智性，是由于其将另一种族裔部分或完全消化并融入自身而被催化，从而使黏液怪吸收了该族裔的一些感受与习性。

由于软泥怪通过分裂进行繁殖，有智性黏液怪并非只是因为某只黏液怪的核心觉醒而随机出现。它们也可以是其他有智性黏液怪的孩子，从出生之刻起便具有意识。我听说，由于黏液怪的智性趋同进化在不同黏液怪之间略有差异，新生黏液怪可能会带着一个几乎是其亲代黏液怪复制品的心智出生，也可能拥有一个高度可塑且尚未发育的心智，如同大多数其他族裔的婴儿，或者完全是别的样子。

我必须承认，有智性黏液怪对我这样的专家而言也仍是一个新研究领域。若你正在阅读这些文字，我希望你能与我一同继续研究它们，让我们能理解更多它们的奥秘。它们不同寻常的存在，甚至可能掌握着研发药物的关键，帮助我们所有人过上更健康的生活。

——艾瑟瑞亚·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的倡导者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
In Defense of Slimes

Contrary to the belief of those who wish to mock my views with a straw man argument, I don’t believe that inanimate or truly mindless beings belong in the conversation about “people” and “monsters.” While there is some complexity involved when it comes to vitae, or so-called nature spirits, which can embody even a shrub or a rock and allow a spellcaster to converse with them, nonetheless, it’s easier to think of those vitae as separate but connected expressions of the world’s life force. When it comes to completely mindless creatures, they don’t even usually have that much. It might surprise the uninformed that it’s possible to even live and act without a mind.

But life finds ingenious paths through every possibility. In the case of such beings that lack a centralized mind, each portion of the creature’s body serves a purpose towards its conditioned activities, serving as a composite replacement for a brain or mind. Such is the case with the vast majority of oozes, and it is this exact distribution that makes it hard to strike for an ooze’s vitals. If every ounce of goo within its body can perform all of the same bodily functions as the rest, then quantity matters over quality. It’s an interesting defense mechanism against injury, but it ultimately precludes the development of a mind, except for in rare cases. There are some oozes, however, which develop a “nucleus” of sorts, for lack of a better word, a core within their body capable of more than the rest of the ooze. We’ve come to call those oozes that have a nucleus “slimes.” Slimes are capable of more complex behaviors and special abilities than other oozes, depending on what roles their nuclei develop to fill within the slime’s biology. Still, most of them remain mindless… but some slime nuclei develop into their equivalent of brains, granting the slime a conscious sapient mind. These are initially not as powerful as slimes, whose nuclei provide a more directly applicable, spectacular, or magical ability, but they wind up becoming much more powerful over time because they can learn. They can grow, and their nuclei can gain more features.

Still, they look just like mindless creatures, and most adventurers or travelers are not enlightened enough to be able to tell the difference between an ooze, a slime without sapience, and a sapient slime. Like with so many other things, slimes have been forced to develop a way to bridge the gap for our convenience. Thus, sapient slimes do things like learn how to vocalize our speech, create a rough facsimile of our forms out of goo, and even attempt to use our trappings for which most slimes normally have no need, such as clothing or gender. That’s not to say slimes never desire such things on their own—I know of at least one case where the slime’s nucleus awakening to sapience was catalyzed by a partial or complete digestion of another ancestry into the slime, causing the slime to pick up some of that ancestry’s sensibilities.

Since oozes reproduce by splitting, sapient slimes don’t just appear randomly when a slime’s nucleus awakens. They can also be the children of other sapient slimes, conscious from the moment of their birth. I’ve heard that since the convergent evolution of sapience in slimes is slightly different from slime to slime, the newborn slime might be born with a mind that is mostly a copy of its parent slime, a highly plastic and undeveloped mind like an infant of most other ancestries, or something else entirely.

I must admit, sapient slimes are a new field of study, even for an expert such as myself. If you are reading this, I hope you will continue to study them alongside me, so that we can understand more of their mysteries. Their unusual existence might even hold the key to developing medicines that can help us all live healthier lives.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Reflections of Self.name: 自我映照 Reflections of Self
Reflections of Self.text:
摘自艾瑟瑞娅·尼克斯的档案
自我的映照

在一个过度执着于标签的世界里，我们只能通过理解自身的身份来尝试逃离它们。你到底是谁？你了解自己吗？你真正理解自己吗？你能爱自己吗？

但随之而来的还有一些问题——对我们这些把身份当作盾牌，用以抵御充满痛苦与危险的世界的人而言，也许更加令人不安：我们日复一日、分分秒秒，还是同一个人吗？还是说我们会为了适应情境而改变？哲学家告诉我们，我们总是在戴着无数面具中的一张，并在它们之间轮转以匹配处境；我们把自己藏在语境之中来为自己的动作辩护，同时又完全不愿意对我们如何看待他人给予同样的宽容。

什么是怪物，而你是怪物吗？一听到这个问题，你的身份就会立刻帮你把它顶回去。“当然，我不是怪物，”你也许会这么想——除非你把自己也视作怪物，或有一道焦虑的低语提醒你：等等，你也许就是。于是你可能会继续用一种确定不会把你包含在内的方式去定义怪物。这是标签与身份的问题。

但如果你的身份本身并不那么界限分明呢？我们戴上面具，改变我们自身的一点一滴。然而对某些人来说，这些面具——这些改变——远比哲学家的隐喻更为深刻。

于是我们来到变形怪——被厌憎、被不信任、并被当作怪物对待。只要有人低声提及变形怪的存在，就足以引发偏执与阴谋论，并侵蚀信任。你最先想到的也是这个吗？但归根结底，这种发自本能的反应，源于变形怪带给我们的感受。关于他们的一切，都由非变形怪来定义，以便将他们他者化。甚至他们的名字，意为“行走的双重者”，也只是以“不是变形怪的人”为参照来定义他们。对我们而言，他们是“分身”、是“第二个”、是“复制品”。

但变形怪并不只是他人的仿制品。尽管他们总在变化，有时甚至变化剧烈；尽管他们有时会成为面具本身。确实，也许正因为我们对变形怪应当是什么的期待，才会有一些人卷入复杂曲折的阴谋，涉及冒充与替换。又或许，是因为某些变形怪热爱此类阴谋，才让其他人建立起这些期待。但这并不是变形怪所能成为之人的极限。变形怪需要时间才能充分发展他们的世界观，正如他们需要时间才能掌握他们的变形能力。变形怪，尤其是那些仍处在学习“我是谁”与“我能做什么”的早期阶段者，拥有无限的潜力，足以成长到超越社会的期待。他们尚未适应社会期待他们戴上的面具，也未将那些角色视为命中注定。有些这样的变形新手，在学会天衣无缝地完善自己的第一个替代形态后，会以寻找冒险作为了解世界的方式，并藉此认识自己。有些人甚至大胆到不以替代形态出行，而以他们美丽的自然之躯出发，向世界高声宣告：他们是变形怪。愿我们有朝一日都能如此勇敢。世界也会因此而更好。

—— 艾瑟瑞娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Reflections of Self

In a world so overly focused on labels, we can only try to escape them by understanding our own identity. Who are you, really? Do you know yourself? Do you truly understand yourself? Can you love yourself?

But with these questions come others that are perhaps more unsettling for those of us who cling to our identity like a shield against a world full of pain and danger: Are we even the same person day to day, moment to moment? Or do we change to fit the context? Philosophers tell us that we are always wearing one of countless masks and cycling between them to match our situation, cloaking ourselves in context to justify our actions while simultaneously being completely unwilling to extend that same courtesy to how we conceive of others.

What is a monster, and are you a monster? Your identity helps you push back on this question as soon as you hear it. "Of course, I’m not a monster," you might think, unless perhaps you conceive of yourself as one or have a whispering voice of anxiety telling you that wait, you might be one. Then you might go on to define monsters in a way that is certain not to include you in it. It’s a question of labels and identity.

But what if your identity itself wasn’t so clear-cut? We wear our masks and change little bits of who we are. For some, however, those masks—those changes— run far deeper than the philosopher’s metaphors.

And so we come to doppelgangers—despised, mistrusted, and treated as monsters. The mere whisper of the presence of doppelgangers is enough to warrant paranoia and conspiracies and to erode trust. Is that the first thing you thought of too? But ultimately, this visceral reaction is about how doppelgangers make us feel. Everything about them has been defined by non-doppelgangers in order to other them. Even their name, meaning "double goer," purely defines them with respect to the person who isn’t a doppelganger. To us, they are the "double," the "second," the "copy."

But doppelgangers are more than just a facsimile of someone else. Even though they are always changing, sometimes dramatically so, even though they can, at times, become the mask. True, perhaps because of our expectations of what doppelgangers are, there are some who engage in convoluted schemes involving impersonation and replacement. Or maybe it’s because of some doppelgangers’ love for such schemes that others have built up these expectations. But that’s not the limit to who a doppelganger can be. It takes time for a doppelganger to fully develop their worldview, just as it takes time for them to master their shapeshifting abilities. Doppelgangers, particularly those in their early stages of learning about who they are and what they can do, have unlimited potential to grow past what society expects. They haven’t acclimated themselves to the masks society expects of them or accepted those roles as preordained. Some such metamorphic novices, after learning to seamlessly perfect their first alternate form, seek adventure as a way of learning about the world and, through it, learn about themselves. Some are even bold enough to head out not in an alternate form but in their beautiful natural body, to shout out to the world that they are a doppelganger. We should all be so brave someday. The world would be better for it.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

The Youngest Ancestry.name: 最年轻的族裔 The Youngest Ancestry
The Youngest Ancestry.text:
摘自艾瑟瑞亚·尼克斯的档案
最年轻的族裔

我们世界中的许多族裔都有自远古以来便延续至今的古老文化。以库乌之息起誓，我研究过的族裔里，超过一半都拥有其传奇与历史，甚至早于神祇降临于世！这是共同的经历的一部分，而这些故事塑造了我们是谁。它们让我们能以一种更容易吸收而非抗拒的方式，将价值观、信仰与习俗一代代传下去。但并非每个族裔都如此古老。确实，我们世界里有一个族裔新到甚至连我祖母都还在世时，他们才刚刚诞生。

他们称自己为斯忒诺。你也许会认出他们看起来很像美杜莎，而他们的相似之处不止于此。在这个世界里，存在着一种极其强大的美杜莎，被称为欧律阿勒，她们受到恶魔力量的诅咒，同时也因此得势。某位这样的欧律阿勒在一位女神祝福的帮助下反抗了自己的命运；有人说，是春之女神奇拉帮助她开启这段新生；也有人说，是艺术与变革女神莉琳帮助她改变了命运。以这类事情的尺度而言，这发生在相当近期：不到一个世纪之前。从这位反抗命运的欧律阿勒的蛇发中，萌发出了一个在此世界前所未见的新族裔。那一百条蛇各自成长为不同的人，他们之间差异多样；的确，女神的祝福是强大之物。

然而，最有趣的部分发生在之后。并非发生在久远而朦胧的神话过去，文化与社会学的学者们得以欣喜地亲眼见证一个族裔在我们眼前发展出其文化与认同。而这一过程远不如那些历经时代流传下来的故事所暗示的那样整齐划一。斯忒诺已开始发展出丰富多彩的文化织锦。更重要的是，他们显然与人类有足够多的共通之处（而且我推测，也许也与美杜莎有关，尽管我对后者没有证据）能够生育后代，而孩子要么是斯忒诺，要么是人类。这或许是女神出于必要所为，因为仅由100名最初的斯忒诺构成的族群，可能并不足以代表能让其族裔轻易存续所需的多样性。斯忒诺究竟是伟大希望的象征，还是一场悲剧，要取决于境况。由于我们抱持的诸多偏见——我已对此进行了深入探讨——这些好奇、热忱而忠诚的人，常常发现周围的社群对他们感到紧张，甚至公然敌视。有些人被美杜莎著名的石化凝视所驱使而恐惧；另一些人则可能将注意力放在斯忒诺异常的外貌，或围绕蛇类的普遍且不该有的负面刻板印象上。我听说某些强大的斯忒诺确实能在短时间内施展石化凝视，但若你研习法师之道足够久，你也能把人石化。斯忒诺并不比任何人更会危害周围的人。

幸运的是，许多斯忒诺最终会找到充满爱与欢迎他们的社群，并在其中茁壮成长，往往建立起大家庭，结交起庞大的朋友圈。若我们能学会彼此理解，也许总有一天，那会是所有斯忒诺——或至少几乎所有——的常态。眼下，若你恰巧遇见一位斯忒诺，不妨认为自己很走运。和他们聊上几句，你会庆幸自己这么做了。

—— 艾瑟瑞亚·尼克斯，空间元素的传令官，怪物代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
The Youngest Ancestry

So many ancestries in our world have ancient cultures that date back since time immemorial. Kuu’s breath, more than half of those I’ve studied have legends and histories that predate the arrival of the gods themselves! It’s part of the shared experience, and those stories make us who we are. They allow us to pass on values, beliefs, and mores from one generation to the next in ways that we easily absorb rather than reject. But not every ancestry is so ancient. Indeed, one ancestry in our world is so new that even my own grandmother was alive when they were first born.

They call themselves stheno. You might recognize them as looking similar to medusas, and their resemblance doesn’t end there. In this world, there exist extremely powerful medusas known as euryales who are cursed and empowered by demonic forces. One such euryale defied her fate with the help of a goddess’s blessing; some say it was Chira, goddess of spring, who helped her with this new beginning, and others say that it was Lilin, goddess of art and change, who helped her change her fate. This was relatively recent as these things go: it happened less than a century ago. From this defiant euryale’s snake hair sprouted a new ancestry, never before seen in this world. Each of the hundred snakes grew into a different person, with quite the variety between them; truly, a goddess’s blessing is a powerful thing.

However, the most interesting part happened afterwards. Rather than occurring some time ago in the hazy mythic past, cultural and sociological scholars had the distinct joy of seeing an ancestry develop its culture and identity right in front of our eyes. And the process is not nearly so monolithic as the stories that survive the ages would have you believe. Sthenos have begun to develop a rich and varied tapestry of cultures. It helps that they apparently have enough in common with humans (and, I speculate, perhaps also medusas, though I have no proof on the latter) to have children, which are either stheno or human. This may have been out of the goddess’s necessity since a pool of only 100 original sthenos might not have represented enough diversity for their ancestry to easily survive. Sthenos are either a sign of great hope or a tragedy, depending on the circumstances. Due to the many biases we have, which I’ve explored in great depth, these curious, passionate, and loyal people have found communities that tend to be nervous around them, or even openly hostile. Fear of medusas’ famous petrifying gaze motivates some, while others might be focused on sthenos’ unusual appearance or on the general, undeserved negative stereotypes surrounding snakes. I’ve heard that some powerful sthenos are able to muster a petrifying gaze for a short time, but you could petrify someone too if you studied wizardry long enough. Sthenos aren’t a danger to those around them any more than anyone else.

Fortunately, many sthenos eventually find loving and welcoming communities in which they can flourish, often starting big families and cultivating large friend groups. If only we learned to understand each other, someday maybe that will be all sthenos, or almost all at least. For now, if you happen to meet a stheno, consider yourself lucky. Strike up a conversation, and you’ll be glad you did.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

An Object Lesson.name: 物体课 An Object Lesson
An Object Lesson.text:
摘自艾瑟瑞娅·尼克斯的档案
物体教训

我们总是太快把其他人当作物体对待，即便是同一族裔，也会因为任何归根结底微不足道的理由。若一个人不仅是“怪物”，还长得像个物体，那他们又还有什么机会可言？毫无机会。

人们早就知道拟形怪很聪明。经典的宝藏箱伎俩也许只是巧合，是随着时代推移学会的伪装手段，但若你研究古代拟形怪的技巧与能力，就会清楚拟形怪和我们任何人一样聪明。就连怪物研究者也知道，拟形怪通常并不邪恶。那么，这些生物——和你我一样聪明——难道会一辈子坐着不动，只盼你来尝试打开一个箱子，好让它们把你吃掉吗？当然不会。众所周知，它们不喜欢其他拟形怪，但它们会与其他非拟形怪生物交往，甚至包括它们潜在的猎物。通过这种方式，它们是在渴求陪伴。

有些孤独的拟形怪离开洞窟与地城——它们的同类通常称之为家的地方——去探索外面的世界，寻找新的交谈伙伴，这当然不足为奇。而由于冒险者最有可能在这类地区探索，那些成功避开冒险者见之即攻的偏见的拟形怪，有时会收获一段意想不到的友谊。其他冒险者可以为这只拟形怪作证，于是，一个不同寻常的新英雄便诞生了。

大多数拟形怪都会面对一个问题：它们的黏着物让它们慢得离谱。这意味着，一个精灵只需随意迈出一步，就能跑赢普通拟形怪最快的速度。但拟形怪生物学中最重要的性相是适应性。因此，投身冒险的拟形怪早已削弱了自己的黏着物，以提高它们的速度，使其能够跟上队伍。随着经验增长，它们学会如何进一步调整自身形态，先是接近、继而超越学者们最狂野传说中的长者拟形怪。

投身冒险的拟形怪为了能以自己的节奏探索外部世界而使黏着物变性，这一做法蕴含着大量象征意义。这种转变代表着不再愿意被过去、以及它们在地城中所经历的静态体验所黏住并陷入其中。相反，这些拟形怪采取了一种充满希望、面向未来的视角，把目光投向地城之外勇敢新世界里的未来。

——艾瑟瑞娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的倡导者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
An Object Lesson

We're all too quick to objectify other people, even of the same ancestry, for any ultimately trivial reason. What chance does a person have if they're not only a "monster" but also one who looks like an object? None at all.

People have known for ages that mimics are clever. The classic treasure chest gambit could just be a coincidence, a matter of learned camouflage over time, but if you study the techniques and abilities of ancient mimics, it becomes clear that mimics are just as smart as any of us. Even monster researchers know that mimics aren't usually evil. So do these creatures, just as smart as you or I, live their entire lives sitting around and hoping for you to come and try to open a chest so they can eat you? Of course, they don't. They are known to dislike other mimics, but they socialize with other non-mimic creatures, even their prospective prey. In this way, they are crying out for companionship.

It's surely no surprise that some lonely mimics leave the caverns and dungeons their kind typically call home to explore the outside world and find new conversation partners. And since adventurers are the most likely people to be exploring such regions, those mimics who manage to avoid the prejudice among adventurers to attack on sight sometimes find an unlikely friendship. The other adventurers can vouch for the mimic, and thus an unusual new hero is born.

There's one problem most mimics face. Their adhesive makes them incredibly slow. This means an elf can outrun the average mimic's fastest speed with a single casual stride. But the most important aspect of a mimic's biology is adaptability. Thus, adventuring mimics have long since weakened their adhesive in order to increase their speed such that they can keep pace. As they grow in experience, they learn how to adjust their forms even more, approaching and then surpassing the elder mimics of scholars' wildest tales.

There is a great deal of symbolism in the way adventuring mimics denature their adhesive to be able to explore the outside world at their own pace. This transformation represents the willingness to no longer be glued to and mired in the past and the static experiences they found in a dungeon. Instead, these mimics adopt a hopeful, forward-thinking perspective, with eyes on a future in the brave new world outside the dungeon.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Fairy Tales Made Real.name: 让童话成真 Fairy Tales Made Real
Fairy Tales Made Real.text:
摘自艾瑟里娅·妮克斯的档案
童话成真

当心仙灵族。这些都是父母讲给孩子听的故事。那些奇异的生物，外表与某些类人生物族裔相似，尤其是人类与精灵，但其心智却异质得可怖，以至于他们仍是夜里发出怪响的存在。千万别吃仙灵的食物，也别喝他们的酒。你可能会被永远带走，或被带走一整个世纪，等你回来时，世界早已改头换面。他们古老，或许不朽；他们可能会从摇篮里偷走婴儿，再用仙灵换子替换，让其在凡人之中长大。

真相更为复杂。我当然不是说你应该在不加谨慎的情况下信任希德的提议。那些故事存在自有原因。许多希德会做出我们会认为残忍、冷酷或不道德的决定，只因他们对时间、尺度与我们周遭世界的视角不同。但这并不意味着就该一概而论，把所有希德都一竿子打死。希德的仁善与彻底的残酷之间亦有跨度，正如任何其他族裔一样；只是童话的警告往往讲的是他们宫廷中最残忍的那部分。许多更为仁慈或更为超然的希德不太会与凡人互动：要么觉得我们凡人应当不受仙灵政治的干扰而活出自己的人生，要么干脆对我们毫不在意。这就使得那些仅为取乐而玩弄我们的人，成了最常遇见的那一类。

但这并不意味着在凡人世界里只有这些希德。还有所谓的希德“英雄”。听起来像是尊称，但我们这些足够理解仙灵宫廷文字游戏微妙之处的人已经明白，这通常是对相关希德的一种隐晦的侮辱。这些是踏上征途的希德，追求更大的刺激、冒险、知识、力量，或有时甚至是为了帮助他人。这些英雄往往会与各种族裔的同伴结伴，常是其他妖精，也有时来自更遥远的地方，以追逐宏大的使命与目标。鉴于一些最顶尖的学术理论认为，他们可能源自同一种存在性成分——那种成分也催生了对贵族与骑士过度浪漫化的传说（而这些人在现实中常是暴君与杀人犯）——即便最仁善的希德英雄，有时也会显得极度傲慢，常常自认为掌控全局，即使其他人都把行动视作团队合作。看着他与一条日耀巨龙或其他同样傲慢的族裔斗嘴，或许颇为滑稽。然而，希德独特的视角意味着，在我们嘲笑这般景象之前必须谨慎。说到底，希德看起来比拟形怪或黏液怪更像人类，却可能是最异质的存在——这么说很奇怪吗。

— 艾瑟里娅·妮克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Fairy Tales Made Real

Beware the fair folk. These are the stories that parents tell their children. Strange creatures who look similar to certain humanoid ancestries, especially humans and elves, but yet have minds so alien that they are still the things that go bump in the night. Never eat faerie food or drink their libations. You might be spirited away forever, or for a century, only to return to a world transfigured. Ancient and perhaps immortal, they might steal infants out of their cradles and replace them with faerie changelings to be raised among mortalkind.

The truth is more complicated. I'm certainly not saying you should trust a sidhe's offer without being cautious. There are reasons those stories exist. Many sidhe make decisions that we would consider cruel, callous, or immoral because of their perspective on time, scope, and the world around us. But that doesn't make it right to paint all sidhe with the same brush. Sidhe have a range of benevolence to utter cruelty just like any other ancestry, and it's just the case that the fairy tale warnings are told about the cruelest of their courts. Many of the kinder or more aloof sidhe are less likely to interact with mortals, feeling either that we mortals should be allowed to live our lives unhindered by faerie politics or simply not caring about us one way or the other. This leaves those interested in toying with us for their own amusement the most commonly encountered.

But it doesn't mean they are the only sidhe out there in the mortal world. There are also the so-called sidhe "heroes." That sounds like an honorific, but those of us with a deep enough understanding of the nuances of wordplay in the faerie courts have come to understand that it is generally used as a veiled insult toward the sidhe in question. These are questing sidhe who seek greater excitement, adventure, knowledge, power, or sometimes even to help others. These heroes are likely to join up with others of various ancestries, often other fey but sometimes much farther afield, in order to pursue grand quests and objectives. Given that they may have arisen, as far as some of the top scholarly theories suggest, from the same existential component that lead to overly romanticized tales of nobility and knights (often tyrants and murderers in real life), even the most benevolent sidhe hero can come across as extremely arrogant at times, often considering themself to be "in charge" of operations even if everyone else thinks of them as a group effort. It might be humorous to see one banter with a solar dragon or other ancestry that shares their haughty nature. Still, the unique perspective of sidhe means we must be careful before we laugh at such a spectacle. Is it strange to say that sidhe, who look more human by far than a mimic or slime, might be the most alien of all in the end.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Demon's Advocate.name: 恶魔的辩护者 Demon's Advocate
Demon's Advocate.text:
摘自艾瑟里娅·尼克斯的档案
恶魔的辩护者

我知道你一定在问自己：艾瑟里娅是否愿意把她的辩护推进到如此地步，以至于为恶魔所犯下的暴行辩护——而在大多数人的眼中，恶魔就是邪恶的具象化身？我的回答是：是，也不是。把某种“原初”之罪与生俱来地扣在活的生物身上，是极其有害的观念，也是偏见蔓延、扎下丑陋根须的一种方式。但当对象是像恶魔这样在来世中成形的存在时，这种说法却并不完全错误。恶魔由一个或多个灵魂形成，这些灵魂早已度过充满罪孽的一生，并被转化为一种新形态，以契合那渗入其存在本质的罪。他们并非像你一样由物质构成，而是由第五精华构成——你可以将其理解为被转质的灵体或灵魂之“质”。这意味着他们的一部分字面意义上就是罪，而我不是那种在不是真“字面意义”时还会用“字面意义”这个词的人。

那么，在我说完这些之后，你一定会想：我怎么可能还愿意为恶魔辩护？答案是：罪孽的一生并不足以成为强迫某人承受永恒诅咒之苦的理由——尤其当他们在如此漫长之后仍有力量改变自身命运，甚至在其存在的构成所加诸的近乎不可能的锁链之下依然如此。人们并不总能选择自己的人生会走向何处；而多元宇宙严酷而残忍，若要因他们在有限的空间与不确定的处境中所能做到的一切而永远审判他们，那便更是如此。

正因如此，当一个恶魔凭自身自由意志撕碎那些锁链时，才显得更加令人惊讶，也更加令人怀抱希望。恶魔的罪是其存在本身、其身体本身的一部分。通过某种方式从内在汇聚出力量并将罪之锁链扯断，恶魔否定了自身的一项根本部分，也否定了其力量的重要源泉。但他们也因此获得自由——摆脱那种强迫他们成为原本模样的审判：成为永恒恶毒、制造扰乱的罪之泉源。

他们自由之后能做什么？任何他们想做的事！恶魔也许会选择继续像从前一样邪恶，只是再也没有罪的镣铐逼迫他们一次又一次地重复那些动作，仿佛某种宇宙性的惩罚。当然，这种可能性确实存在，我也并不声称所有这类恶魔都是圣徒。但我认为：一个愿意走到剜去自身力量源泉、以夺回自身命运这一步的恶魔，值得尊重，也值得一次机会，让我们看看他们会如何运用这份从命运残酷拥抱中挣得的新自由。我并非请求对所有恶魔都予以宽恕与体谅，而是请求：对于那些点燃自身道路的恶魔，至少给予尊重，并愿意暂缓最初的审判。因为如果我们仍旧预先定罪，唾弃他们为开辟自身道路所作的惊人牺牲，那么我们难道不就只是残酷无情命运的棋子，试图把一种稀有而光辉的存在再度碾回那副邪恶的形状——那副命运替他们选定的形状吗？

— 艾瑟里娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的辩护者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Demon’s Advocate

I know you have to be asking yourself: Is Aitheria willing to take her advocacy so far that she'll defend the atrocities committed by demons, who are, by most accounts, literal incarnations of evil? I'm going to say yes, and no. The concept of being born with some kind of "original" sin is incredibly harmful when applied to living creatures and a way that bigotry spreads its ugly roots. But it's not exactly wrong when it comes to a being formed in the afterlife like a demon. A demon is formed from one or more souls who have already lived a life full of sin and have been transfigured into a new form, befitting the sin that infuses their very being. They are built not of matter like you are but instead of quintessence, which you can think of as transubstantiated spirit or soul stuff. And that means part of them literally is sin, and I'm not the type to use the word literally when I don't mean literally.

So after I said all that, you must be wondering how I can possibly be willing to advocate for demons. And the answer is this: a life of sin is no reason to force someone to suffer an eternity of damnation, especially if they have the strength to change their fate even after so long, and even with the almost impossible chains placed upon them by their being's very composition. People don't always have a choice as to the way their life ends up, and the multiverse is harsh and cruel to judge them eternally for what they could do with finite time and uncertain circumstances.

That's why it's all the more surprising and incredibly hopeful when a demon rips off those chains of their own free will. The demon's sin is a part of their very being, their very body. By somehow mustering something from within themself and ripping the chains of sin loose, a demon denies a fundamental part of themself and the source of much of their power. But they also become free. Free of the judgment that forced them to be as they were, an eternally malevolent and disruptive font of sin.

What can they do once they are free? Whatever they want! The demon might choose to be just as evil as they were before, only without the fetters of sin forcing them to live out those actions over and over as some kind of cosmic punishment. Certainly, that's possible, and I'm not saying all such demons are saints. But I feel a demon who would go so far as to rip out the source of their power to seize their destiny deserves respect and a chance to show us what they will make of their newfound freedom from destiny's cruel embrace. I ask not for clemency and consideration for all demons but instead for respect and a willingness to withhold initial judgment specifically for those demons who blaze their own path. Because if we prejudge them anyway, and spit on the incredible sacrifices they've made in their attempt to forge their own path, then aren't we simply the pawns of cruel and merciless fate, seeking to crush a rare and glorious existence back into the wicked shape it chose for them?

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

The Tangled Maze of \"Monsterhood\".name: “怪物性”的纠结迷宫 The Tangled Maze of "Monsterhood"
The Tangled Maze of \"Monsterhood\".text:
出自艾瑟里娅·尼克斯的档案
“怪物性”的纠结迷宫

究竟是什么让人类以及他们周围的其他人认定你是个怪物？认定你的心智一定更接近野兽，认定你不那么配被称作一个人？正如我先前写过的那样，因素很多，但其中一个相当常见的对应因素，是外表与动物有某些相似之处。大多数人类——好吧，除了那些“邪恶”的人之外——认为在正常情况下杀死另一个人类是错误的。然而，即便如此，仍有许多人喜欢大规模屠宰并吞食动物。于是，那些渴望动物血肉的人，就需要在心中建立一层保护性的帷幕，一种避免认知失调的方式。最容易的办法，就是将动物“他者化”，宣称因为它们更不聪明、缺乏理性，所以它们活该被囚禁、虐待，并作为家畜迎来死亡。不幸的是，对于所有在某些特性上与动物相似的理性族裔而言，这种态度往往会向外蔓延，波及那些看起来像动物的人，于是这些人也会被那些吞食动物血肉者所轻视。

以牛头人作例子。它们是狩猎大师，天生狡黠，使其能够轻松穿行迷宫。它们的文化与任何其他族裔一样丰厚，却仅仅因为外貌便被视为怪物与蛮徒。正是恐惧的村民的偏见与不宽容，将牛头人推向恶魔领主张开的怀抱，从而以残酷的方式应验了人类自己的预言。

但牛头人超越了狭隘的人类想象强加给它们的那些期待。它们拥有古老的根源，因此，到现在你想必也不会惊讶：它们远不止我们的刻板印象所描绘的那般。的确，有些牛头人在迷宫中恐吓他人，但也有一些运用其解开迷宫般谜题的能力来增进理解、追寻启迪，或解决复杂的社会问题。不同职业的牛头人冒险者，都在追寻一个与我们当下所处不同的世界；而另一些则对此不以为意，只想与同伴并肩合作，击倒一些危险威胁，并赢得荣耀。当被逼到只能战或死时，牛头人可以成为可怕的敌人；但当它们被允许蓬勃发展时，牛头人也有许多东西可以与我们其余人分享。问题是，我们会允许它们吗？

—— 艾瑟里娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
The Tangled Maze of "Monsterhood"

What sorts of things make humans and others around them assume that you are a monster? That your mind must be more bestial, someone less deserving of being called a person? As I've written before, there are many factors, but one that corresponds fairly often is an appearance that shares something in common with animals. Most humans, well except the "evil" ones anyway, consider it wrong to kill another human under normal circumstances. But yet, many still love to slaughter and devour animals en masse. This, then, requires those who yearn for the flesh of animals to establish a protective veil in their minds, a way to avoid the cognitive dissonance. The easiest way to do so is by othering animals, claiming that since they are less intelligent and nonsapient, that they deserve captivity, abuse, and death as livestock. Unfortunately, for all the sapient ancestries who share some of their features with animals, this attitude tends to bleed outward toward those resembling animals and are thereby looked down upon by those who devour animal flesh.

Consider minotaurs, for example. They are master hunters with a natural cunning, allowing them to navigate mazes with ease. Their culture is as rich as any other ancestry, yet they are considered monsters and brutes simply because of their appearance. It was fearful villagers' prejudices and intolerance that sent minotaurs to the waiting grasp of demon lords, thus fulfilling the humans' own prophecies in a cruel way.

But minotaurs transcend those expectations placed upon them by narrow human imaginations. Theirs is an ancient tradition, and so surely it should come as no surprise to you by now that they can be so much more than our stereotypes make them out to be. Indeed, some minotaurs terrorize others in mazes, but others use their ability to solve labyrinthine puzzles to further understanding, seek enlightenment, or solve complex societal problems. Minotaur adventurers of all different vocations seek a different world than the one we live in now, while others think little of such things and just want to work together with their comrades, take down some dangerous threats, and earn glory. When pushed to fight or die, minotaurs can be fearsome foes, but when allowed to flourish, minotaurs have much to share with the rest of us. The question is, will we let them?

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

On Beauty and Monsters.name: 论美与怪物 On Beauty and Monsters
On Beauty and Monsters.text:
摘自艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯的档案
论美与怪物

人类是整个世界上最引人入胜的怪物之一。还有什么别的怪物，在面对与自己不同的存在（无论只是略有差异，还是截然不同），但同样具备智慧时，会在其反应动作中如此干脆地分裂成仅有两种可能？要么杀死他们无法理解的东西，要么试图开启一段浪漫关系，中间几乎没有余地。我们选择性地抛出“怪物”这个词，又有什么可奇怪的呢？如果你的道德或文化冒犯了人类，如果你更具攻击性或领地意识，尤其是如果你的审美对人类而言不讨喜或令人反感，你就更可能被贴上怪物的标签并被当场杀死。另一方面，有些族裔会落在亲吻/杀戮光谱的另一端，因为他们体现了相反的特征：拥有一种人类容易尊重的道德或文化，通常对不具威胁的新来者更为仁善，或尤其在外表上令人愉悦且富有吸引力。那些为一己议程而滥用“怪物”一词的人类学者会宣称，这样的生物根本算不上怪物——而这恰恰说明，这个词是由基于人类凝视的共识来决定的，而不是通过从每个族裔自身的视角去理解他们。毫无价值。

无论如何，没有哪个族裔能比宁芙更能体现这一教训——她们是自然之美的化身与守护者。听着，如果你被宁芙吸引，我不评判你。谁会不被各种性别的宁芙吸引呢？那多少就是她们的一部分。但让我们越过这一点，谈谈那些四处游历、与其自然守护之地切断羁绊以换取自由的冒险宁芙。你或许曾在某个时候遇到过一位，但她们相对罕见。我希望你忘掉你脑海里那位宁芙的形象。相反，把美放到一边。在这里，我们面对的是一个异界的存在——一位被赋予守护职责、必须守卫其守护之地的保护者，却在追寻另一条道路。她们真的就与冒险的恶鬼有那么不同吗？那么，为什么人类聚居地会觉得恶鬼是不可救赎的存在，而冒险的宁芙却是令人愉悦的访客？恶鬼的过去有一次失败，而“人们”（另一个充满偏见的词）总爱编造叙事，把某个曾经失败过一次的人（无论是否是他的错）归咎为其当下处境的原因；看看我们如何对待曾经成瘾的人或其他脆弱群体就知道了。恶鬼往往更具攻击性，而且确实，其中许多人会为获得更多力量而接受与邪恶的契约。最后，恶鬼的真实形态可能令人望而生畏。而这最后一点，是大众真正了解他们的少数信息之一。的确，我们可以很肤浅。

这一切也并非意在贬低宁芙冒险者的成就——无论是在战斗中，还是在担任使节时。族群之间的关系是双方共同的责任，而在这一点上，宁芙表现卓越。精类拥有异于常人的视角与道德观，而宁芙尤其更眷恋自然世界，胜过凡人的社会。但她们仍能充分理解人类心智的运作方式，足以融入其中。而我所谓的“融入”，并不是指无缝地伪装成其中一员；宁芙无论走到哪里都很显眼。我的意思是，她们能够滑入人类理解与文化中的某种角色，让人们以有利的方式看待她们——不将其视为“怪物”，而视为某种美丽、神秘、被误解之物。

——艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的倡导者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
On Beauty and Monsters

Humankind is one of the most fascinating monsters in the entire world. What other monster, when presented with someone different (whether only slightly different or dramatically so) but no less sapient, would split so cleanly in their reactions between just two possibilities? Either murdering what they didn't understand or trying to start up a romantic relationship with little middle ground. Is it any wonder that we selectively throw the word "monster"? If your morality or culture offends humanity, if you're more aggressive or territorial, or especially if your aesthetics are unappealing or repulsive to humans, you're more likely to be labeled a monster and killed on sight. On the other hand, some ancestries wind up on the other side of the kiss/kill spectrum because they embody the opposite traits: possessing a morality or culture that humans can easily respect, generally benevolent to non-threatening newcomers, or especially aesthetically appealing and attractive. Human scholars who misuse the term "monster" for their own agenda would claim that such a creature is no monster at all, which simply shows how the term is decided by the consensus based on the human gaze rather than by understanding each ancestry by its own perspective. Worthless.

In any case, no other ancestry quite embodies this lesson more than nymphs, the very incarnations and protectors of natural beauty. Look, I'm not judging you if you're attracted to nymphs. Who wouldn't be attracted to nymphs of all genders? That's kind of part of who they are. But let's look past that and talk about the wandering adventurous nymphs who severed the bond with their natural ward to earn their freedom. You might have met one at some point, but they're relatively rare. I want you to forget your mental image of that nymph. Instead, set the beauty aside. Here we have an otherworldly being, a protector charged to guard a ward, who is pursuing a different path. Are they really that different from adventuring oni? Then why do human settlements feel that oni are irredeemable beings while adventuring nymphs are pleasant visitors? Oni have a failure in their past, and "people" (another loaded word) love to invent narratives blaming someone who failed once (whether or not it was their fault) for their current circumstances; just see how we treat former addicts or other vulnerable people. Oni tend to be more aggressive, and it's true many of them accept a pact with evil for more power. And lastly, oni in their true form can be terrifying to behold. And that last is one of the few things the masses really know about them. Indeed we can be shallow.

None of that is meant to minimize the accomplishments of nymph adventurers, either in battle or in ambassadorship. A relationship between peoples is the responsibility of both sides, and in that sense, nymphs have excelled. Fey have alien perspectives and moralities, and nymphs, in particular, are more attached to the natural world than the societies of mortals. But they can still understand how the human mind works well enough to fit in. And by fit in, I don't mean blend in seamlessly; nymphs stand out wherever they go. I mean they can slide into a role in human understanding and culture that casts them in a beneficial light as seen not as a "monster" but as something beautiful, mysterious, and misunderstood.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Gremlins in the Gears.name: 道具中的小魔怪 Gremlins in the Gears
Gremlins in the Gears.text:
摘自艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯的档案
道具里的小魔怪

厄运。诅咒。坏运气护符。想象一下，每当有人犯了错，或事情出了岔子，就怪到你头上，那会是什么感觉。当一件原型装置发生故障，或一件老而可靠的工具坏掉时——哦，那可不是我们的错。只是道具里有小魔怪罢了。若这种责难、这种对个人责任的推卸，频繁地落到你我身上，无疑会令人沮丧、并使人失去人性。对于妖精生灵而言——那些与自然世界的原初魔法有着强烈联结、并在代代相传的童话与故事之间架起桥梁的生物——其效果要深刻得多。因为归根结底，即便其他人一直把他们的失误怪到你我头上，他们爱怎么说就怎么说，也能影响公众舆论，但那终究不会成为事实。然而对小魔怪而言，正如其他妖精一样，他们的信念与与他人的联结，本就是其“真实”的一部分。因此，随着时间推移，人形生物那种总想把我们的失败归咎于他人的倾向，像形而上的污泥或下水道的秽物一般滚下山坡，最终倾倒在小魔怪身上，将邪恶的力量与恶毒的意图灌注给他们，以符合最初被捏造出来的那些借口。

而到了那一步，它便成了一个自我延续的循环。随着小魔怪越来越被这些故事中转移来的恶意所侵染，这些故事在传播得更远时也变得更有力量，而小魔怪则更深地沉入污秽之中。这一过程催生了各种各样的传说变体，进而让无数小魔怪传承在时光中相继出现。就这样，小魔怪成了我们在虚荣地急于为失败开脱、并让自己继续当自己故事主角时所造就的怪物。难怪小魔怪如此乐于折磨人形生物？难怪他们似乎几乎以恶作剧为食？这并非传统意义上他们为了活而必须摄取的养分；但以一种间接的方式，且在缓慢的时间流逝中，随着我们的痛苦传播着关于小魔怪及其力量的故事，他们确实会因之而兴盛。

在这种有毒的文化之下，小魔怪若想打破成见，又怎会不难以被人形生物社会接纳？他们不仅被当作“怪物”而遭到排斥；他们更被字面意义上塑造成替罪羊、背罪者，为所谓“文明”社会及其种种外衣的所有缺陷吞下罪过。周围充斥着那些童话与迷信时，你可想而知，小魔怪想找到一个能称之为“家”的地方有多困难。考虑到我们关于他们所说的一切——我们把他们塑造成的模样——又有谁会接纳一只活在他们之间的小魔怪？即便是一只辛勤证明自己确为英雄的小魔怪，起初也许会被勉强接纳；但你觉得，一旦东西停止运作，谁会立刻成为被怪罪与被怀疑的对象？即便是那些对小魔怪抱有同情、并相信小魔怪并无恶意的人，也很可能在责怪小魔怪的同时，还相信小魔怪“不是故意的”或“就是忍不住”，觉得问题的根源在于他们的存在本身，而不管小魔怪是否出于自愿。这便是有关好运或霉运之迷信的力量，也是我们认知方式的一处缺陷：它试图在互不相干的事件背后编织叙事，以制造并不存在的解释。时间与世界是残酷的盗贼。随着时间推移，即便是一件精雕细琢、无价的杰作也会破损。而当它们破碎时，多数情况下，确实不会有任何可辨识的理由说明为什么会发生。我只希望，当那件无价且破碎的宝藏是一只小魔怪的心时，我也能如此断言。

— 艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物倡导者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Gremlins in the Gears

Jinxes. Curses. Bad luck charms. Imagine what it must feel like to be blamed whenever someone makes a mistake, or something goes wrong. When a prototype device malfunctions or an old reliable tool breaks down, oh, it wasn't our fault. It was just gremlins in the gears. If that sort of blame, the abrogation of personal responsibility, was heaped with regularity on you or me, it would surely be demoralizing and dehumanizing. For fey beings, creatures with a strong connection that bridges the gap between the primal magic of the natural world and the fairy tales and stories we pass down over generations, the effect is much more profound. Because ultimately, if other people keep blaming you or me for their mishaps, they can do so all they want and influence public opinion, but it will still never be true. But for gremlins, like other fey, their beliefs and connections to others are part of their truth. And so, over time, the humanoid tendency to want to blame someone else for our failures has rolled down the hill like metaphysical sludge or sewage until it landed on gremlins, infusing them with fell powers and malevolent intentions to match the very excuses conjured up in the first place.

And at that point, it becomes a self-perpetuating cycle. As gremlins become more and more possessed by the shifted malice of these stories, the stories become more potent as they spread further, and gremlins sink even deeper into the muck. This process spawned all sorts of variations on the tales, leading to countless heritages of gremlins arising over time. In this way, gremlins have become the monsters we made in our vainglorious rush to justify our failures and remain the protagonists of our own stories. Is it any wonder that gremlins delight in tormenting humanoids so? That they seem to almost feed off their mischief? It isn't sustenance in the traditional sense that they need it to live, but in an indirect way and slowly over time, they truly do prosper as our suffering spreads tales of gremlins and their power.

Given this toxic culture, is it at all surprising how difficult it is for gremlins who seek to break the mold to be accepted in humanoid society? They weren't just othered as "monsters," they were literally made the scapegoat, the sin eater, for all the failings of a so-called "civilized" society and the trappings thereof. With all those fairy tales and superstitions around, imagine how hard it is for gremlins to simply find a place to call their own. Given what we have said about them—what we have made them—who would accept a gremlin living among them? Even a gremlin who toiled to prove their bona fides as a hero might be accepted begrudgingly at first, but who do you think is going to immediately take the blame and suspicion the minute things stop working? Even those with some sympathy for the gremlin and who believe that the gremlin doesn't mean any harm are likely to blame the gremlin while believing that the gremlin "isn't doing it on purpose" or "just can't help it," feeling their very presence is responsible for the problems, regardless of the gremlin's volition. This is the power of superstitions involving good or bad luck and one of the flaws in how our cognition tries to spin narratives behind unconnected events to create explanations that don't exist. Time and the world are cruel thieves. Over time, even an exquisitely crafted, priceless masterpiece will break. And when they break, in most cases, there truly won't be any discernible reason why it happened. I only wish I could say the same when the priceless, broken treasure is a gremlin's heart.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Demon Ancestry.name: 恶魔族裔 Demon Ancestry
Demon Ancestry.text:
Demons are formed from the sins of fractious and malevolent souls, some would say destined to wallow in their sins and spread destruction and corruption for all eternity. But some demons escape the pull of that fate at great cost, expending most of their demonic power to write their own destinies. These renegade demons share many similarities to their Abyss-bound kin, but they have the potential to become great heroes and villains in their own right someday, able to challenge even the mightiest balors and win.

It takes an unbreakable will or other extreme circumstances to convince a demon to reject a portion of their fundamental nature as large as their sin. Every renegade demon has their own story, and each of those stories is extremely interesting, rife with unique circumstances and powerful motivations. The process of first casting off the sin varies from renegade demon to renegade demon, but no matter what, when they emerge having rejected their sin, they have an unparalleled control over their own future but significantly weakened demonic powers.

Even so, it's not so easy to leave their sins in the past. Demons must be constantly vigilant lest the chains of the Abyss find a way to worm their way back around their souls. It helps for them to have an obsession, pursuit, or cause. Many of them take up adventuring, whether as a means to an end to maintain their own path, for adventuring's own rewards, or some kind of mix of the two.

If you want to play a being formed of sin and ultimate evil who strives to prove that you can be something more, or carve your own path into the multiverse, you should play a demon.

You Might...

  • Pursue an eccentric obsession as a way to fill the gap inside you from rejecting your demonic power.
  • Struggle with temptation or going too far, especially when it's associated with your original sin.
  • Have to work ten times as hard to earn even a fraction of the trust that mortals place in each other.

Others Probably...

  • Assume that you are a typical demon who is attempting to pull off a particularly poorly-conceived charade.
  • Experience a fight or flight response when they first see you.
  • Believe you are more powerful than you actually are and trust that strength, if not your intentions.

Physical Description

Demons have appearances so diverse between heritages that it would be almost impossible to identify individuals as demons rather than some other creature definitively, were it not for countless legends of their evil deeds, complete with textual descriptions and illustrations. Demons are the ultimate things that go bump in the night, as well as the subject of moral allegories guiding mortals away from a life of sin. Renegade demons sometimes have an aspect that defies the expectations for a normal demon of their heritage, but more often, the two are visually indistinguishable from one another, and it is only through actions that the renegade demon can prove themselves distinct from their brethren trapped in the throes of sin. Mariliths are an exception, as a distinctive part of a normal marilith's physiology (their six arms) depends on their sin of pride, thus causing renegade mariliths to look obviously different. The following are physical descriptions of each heritage, with a few ideas of ways in which an individual might vary if they are one of the rarer renegade demons who are visually distinctive.

Most renegade babaus, like normal babaus, appear as skinless demonic figures dripping red with blood, with sharp teeth, long nails on their hands and feet, a barbed tail, and a single horn. Renegade babaus with a distinctive appearance sometimes are a different color than red, possess less vicious- looking hands and feet, have a different kind of tail, or even have a layer of skin that makes them look far less demonic until the presence of too much gore stimulates their cast-off sin, causing their body to weep blood from every pore.

Renegade brimoraks tend to share the appearance of their Abyssal kin, with sooty goat heads and horns, red eyes, and smoking hooves. Not all of them carry flaming swords, though it's not an uncommon aesthetic choice for brimoraks who can afford such magic. Those with appearances belying the norm might have different color goat fur or eyes; the goat head is constant even then, but they might pass as a different goat-headed creature.

Renegade dretches typically have tiny horns and long downward-pointed ears like cows, sharp teeth like sharks, claws like a mole's, and a distended torso, all features they share in common with more typical dretches. Those who buck the trend tend to lack one or more of these features.

Hezrou renegades, as is typical for hezrous, resemble a cross between a giant toad and a gorilla, with jagged rocks, moldy fungi, and bloated warts growing along their back. Those who vary from this norm still tend to have the basic body shape of a hulking toad; more often what grows off their back is different, with fewer toxic plants or menacing stones. More hezrou renegades have variant appearances than other heritages because, to an extent, they can curate the contents of their backs, and a fair number choose to do so as an art form and venue of self-expression.

Marilith renegades are the most distinctive from their brethren in the Abyss. Pride demons normally manifest up to six arms from their sin of pride, but renegades who abandon that sin possess two normal arms, with up to four spectral arms occasionally appearing in certain circumstances while their confidence in themselves remains high. They otherwise appear as mariliths, with a human upper body and a snake lower body, but their snake lower body is slightly shorter than other mariliths, causing them to be medium-sized. When they're feeling uncertain, they might be mistaken for a lamia matriarch or other ophidian-tailed creature. Those renegades who take this distinctiveness even further might have unusual features adorning their human upper half, but they all possess a serpentine lower body.

Renegade succubi generally keep the usual aesthetic, with a long sinuous tail, bat wings, and a pair of horns with an especially alluring humanoid body. Those who have an unusual feature might lack one of those features, often wings, since a renegade succubus's wings aren't sufficient to hold them aloft without the power of the sin of lust to buoy them. Some retain those features but have forms or visages that don't conform to the traditional standards of beauty, embodying the adage that true beauty comes from within.

Renegade vrocks usually look like Abyssal vrocks, with vulture heads, dark wings, and crimson bodies, and sinuous tails. Those who vary from that appearance still usually maintain their vulture heads, but they might either have wings with a different feather pattern and coloration or different skin tones.

Defining Your Style

The list of sins that can form a demon is as varied as it is enigmatic. Some demons are formed from sins that represent corrupt internal qualities, like lust or sloth, which many societies find immoral. Other sins, however, are much more specific in the actions they embody, such as arson or pollution.

Society

Renegade demons each walk their own path, and their obsession with freedom and choosing their own fate means that they rarely form official societal groups of like-minded renegades. That being said, there are five unofficial divisions, called hosts by some sociologists who study renegades, that explain some of the differences in the ways they relate to themselves and the world around them. Equally as important to a renegade's host is their heritage, as each demon heritage has a different associated sin from whose thrall the renegade is attempting to escape. When you combine the tendencies between their heritage and their host, you have a slightly better chance at understanding how a renegade will act and why they might act that way, but even then, they are highly individualistic and often defy such a simplistic categorization. Remember, just because they've unchained themselves from their sin, that doesn't mean they've abandoned its trappings entirely (only reversers attempt to do so).

Hosts

The following are descriptions for the five most common hosts among renegade demons, followed by the six heritages.

Free Spirits

There's an infinite number of things to do out there in the multiverse, and free spirits want to try all sorts, unburdened by the prescriptive anchor of sin that forced them to act in a certain way. To a free spirit, renegade demons are the truest expression of the triumph of chaos, as they believe the nature of demons being tied to sin and evil is inherently lawful and structured. The fact that demons can successfully become renegades and become something new shows how powerful freedom and chaos are in a multiverse that's otherwise, to their eyes, bogged down with a literally infinite amount of conformity. Unlike many of the other hosts, which might take actions in a special way that relates to their old sin, free spirits feel that the ultimate expression of freedom from their sin is to just ignore those shackles, cast them aside, and outgrow them as something that simply doesn't matter anymore. Do they occasionally perform actions that might be associated with their former sin? Sure. Avoiding it completely would be its own kind of shackle, but even so, they do so no more than any ordinary mortal or other creature that doesn't have a supernatural connection to the sin. Free spirits get into heated debates about this with reversers, as they feel that reversers are still essentially letting their sins rule them by choosing to do literally the opposite. When not being dragged into these debates, free spirits are the least tied to the same struggles their brethren experience. True, they're still ostracized until they present overwhelming proof that their nature isn't some sort of trick, but they can avoid that by using disguises or simply traveling either alone or among those with whom they share a mutual trust. Thus, while free spirits may be capricious and whimsical, they treat true friends as more valuable than gold, and always let their friends know this through both words and deeds. Other than that, they can be found in every environment performing every kind of action imaginable. The world is their oyster, and it's time to have the most fantastic oyster bake anyone has ever seen!

Free spirits are almost always chaotic alignments, and not just slightly chaotic either! Their flamboyantly chaotic nature is a sight to behold. A few are true neutral, embracing the aspect of neutrality that just doesn't care about alignment and wants to do their own thing. There are essentially no free spirits of the other five lawful and neutral alignments.

Redeemed

Most renegade demons prefer that term to describe demons who turned their back on their abyssal sin to forge their own destiny, but redeemed would rather be called redeemed demons. They feel it has a more positive connotation, while correctly pointing out that redeemed demons have risen above the shackles of sin. The reason why renegade demons of other hosts don't follow this convention is twofold: first, the verb redeem is generally transitive, implying an external approach to redemption, while many renegades feel that their main impetus was internal. Second, a significant number of renegades (especially renegade sinners) don't feel that they really are or have been "redeemed." They simply made a choice to live their own lives. These debates can lead to tension between redeemed and other renegades, but ultimately, redeemed want to befriend their fellows and show solidarity, so they are the ones to back down and turn the other cheek. The joy that comes from helping others, especially redeeming them and helping them improve their lives, is the most important feeling to a redeemed demon. It's what lets them banish the call of their sin to the farthest corners of their being until it withers and dies, leaving them no more inclined toward that sin than any other ancestry. Or at least, that's the dream for the redeemed. Redeemed don't believe that being preachy, sententious, or judgmental will ever help them to redeem others. They've experienced one of the most startling and fundamental redemptions possible as beings formerly composed of sin, so that can help give them a perspective on what works and what doesn't from their own situation. One can redeem by leading by example with unfailing kindness, compassion, and forgiveness. Some of the most successful redeemed are succubi, since the aforementioned skills are easily perfected from reversed versions of their old temptation techniques. For this reason, succubus redeemed are sometimes also reversers, believing that by redeeming others they can act precisely in opposition to the nature of their sin.

Unlike reversers, most redeemed don't see any reason to avoid the trappings associated with their sin, even though they broke free from the sin's control over them. For instance, some redeemed brimoraks use fire to help others, some redeemed babaus use bloodletting for medicine, some redeemed hezrous use poisons to treat disease, and some redeemed succubi use pleasure to help others. Of course, just like any other ancestry, sometimes redeemed can go too far, becoming fanatics in their obsession with escaping the sins of the past. However, those who swing the pendulum too far in the opposite direction are the minority among redeemed.

Redeemed are never evil, and usually good. While they can be of any non-evil alignment, neutral good is especially common, and, of course, they find themselves drawn to the redeemer champion cause for obvious reasons.

Demon Adventurers

Renegade demon adventurers prefer an especially wide range of classes, which varies by heritage and host. Few choose to pursue classes linked to the Abyss, such as demonic sorcerer or demon summoner, though sometimes those powers don't offer the renegade demon much of a choice. Redeemed, unsurprisingly, are often redeemer champions. renegade sinners and free spirits like classes such as bard, rogue, and swashbuckler, while regulators prefer classes that offer more structure, such as monk or wizard. Renegade demons who turn to adventuring right after going renegade don't necessarily have much in the way of a prior background, so they tend to build themselves a background that matches their adventuring career as they go (for instance, criminal for a rogue who pursues a life of crime or student of magic for a wizard who studies magic). Otherwise, they have backgrounds that match their role as a demon, such as warrior for combat-oriented demons, emissary for a social infiltrator like a succubus, or even haunted for demons haunted by the spirits of their former victims.

Regulators

Regulators take an unusual approach among renegade demons, but one with a strong underpinning in mortal culture with examples like asceticism or diets. They create for themselves a system of rules, limitations, and regulations in order to curb the influence of their sin and allow them to build a new life step by step, metaphorical brick by metaphorical brick, until they actualize the self they want to become. It's easy to see why this sort of disciplined approach is unlikely among other renegade demon hosts, as it has a decidedly lawful bent to it.

Nevertheless, it's surprisingly effective for those demons who are able to keep to the methodology without riding the slippery slope into creating exceptions or excuses to backslide "just this once." Regulators don't attempt to push any of their rules or limitations on others. Indeed, to them there would be no point, as the very reason that they created such strict rules in the first place was extremely personal. They set down the edicts they chose in order to become the person they desired.

Having others follow those exact strictures would be honestly creepy to most regulators, as it would be an uncanny form of replication of the regulator's own identity. For this reason, combined with their experience as beings of absolute chaos, regulators have a reputation as some of the most tolerable lawful people to hang out with in chaotic circles. They can still be a stick in the mud who'll never have their own fun, but they won't rain on anyone else's parade. On the other hand, if someone asks a regulator to help them create their own personalized set of regulations, they often become excited and are a massive help to those dealing with issues less existential than overthrowing the yoke of absolute sin. Once you have your own personal strictures and goals, asking a regulator to keep you honest can be both the greatest possible blessing, and also the greatest curse. As mellow as they might be about others' actions in most circumstances, once you've enlisted them as a confederate to keep you on your own path, regulators mercilessly pursue that role. In fact, the closer they are to you and the more they care about you, the stricter and more unforgiving they become. Thus, it's crucial to only ask for that particular assistance from a regulator if you're completely serious about achieving your goals.

Needless to say, regulators are almost always lawful in their alignment. Some aren't, though; these non-lawful regulators don't necessarily like rules and laws, they just feel it's the only way for them to be who they want to be, and they likely go through periods of rebellion against their own self-imposed rules, bouncing around behaviors more similar to other hosts, especially renegade sinners.

Renegade Sinners

Renegade sinners are selfish, egotistical, and often vile and cruel, but above all, they are free. These malicious characteristics are solely their own. They've freed themselves from the cycle of sin and the draw of their inherent vice, only to remain evil in the end. Abyssal demons are most perplexed by renegade sinners of all renegade demons. Those possessed of abyssal sin despise everything redeemed represent, but they can intellectually understand why such a repugnant being, who turned away from the path of evil, might choose to become a renegade. But why in the world do renegade sinners go to such extreme efforts, surrendering so much of their abyssal power, if they were going to remain evil anyway? For renegade sinners, it's all about free will and choice. They want to be in control of their own destiny, even if they ultimately use that choice for evil, and to them, the cost is minor compared to the payoff. Besides, decoupling themselves from their sins and becoming renegades allows them to advance in other ways, and that allows them to eventually become stronger than before, especially if they were a weaker demon before. Others become renegade sinners after being defeated as an abyssal demon due to their sin vulnerability and later becoming obsessed with that vulnerability and their sin as an albatross holding them back from their true potential.

Renegade sinners are nearly always evil, though they might also be a particularly selfish neutral, closer to evil than good. Even so, their evil is more likely to be selfish and self-centered rather than massive sadistic murders, rampaging violence, or other such otherworldly evil that one might expect from demons. Renegade sinners can almost always be reasoned with when making an argument in their own self-interest, as they aren't beholden to their sin and the destructive urges it enforces.

Reversers

Reversers live by a simple truth: to fully reject their sin, it's easier for them to act in a way that's the exact opposite rather than simply setting the sin aside and ignoring it entirely. By going out of their way to reinforce their opposition to their sin, they enforce the contradiction and strengthen their defenses. If they didn't, reversers fear that they would slide down a slippery slope, re- embrace their sin, and lose their renegade status before they knew it. What it means to be a reverser varies from heritage to heritage, and even among the same heritage, different demons sometimes have different ideas about what it means to take their sin and reverse it to its opposite. Succubus reversers in particular are notorious for having various camps, all of which are convinced that their interpretation is the most elemental reversal of their sin. Nonetheless, most reversers agree that it doesn't matter whose reversal is objectively "correct," as it matters more whether an individual reverser's chosen turnabout resonates with their own personality and circumstances.

More so than the other hosts, reversers can vary among alignments, though few are evil. The variation also depends on the reverser's heritage, as reversing some sins have more alignment implications than others. For instance, dretches who reverse their sloth can use that vigorous activity for all sorts of ends and are especially likely to vary across alignments, while reverser hezrou seeking to heal and uncorrupt are very likely to be good-aligned.

Demon Settlements

Renegade demons are few and far between, but they have a way of finding each other, whether it's by instinct or shared tendencies. Thus, small settlements of renegade demons tend to pop up in hub locations like the Astral Plane, as well as in Material Plane locations that have enough abyssal influence to see a large population of abyssal demons. Others live in isolation, realizing it will be difficult for society to accept that a demon means no harm. Those with either sufficient skills at disguise or enough notoriety to earn tolerance, or even acceptance, try their luck living in mortal settlements. While in good times, this can be a fruitful existence for a renegade demon with a good reputation, unfortunately, it doesn't take much for most mortals to begin to suspect their resident demon whenever unexplained misfortunes begin cropping up.

Heritages

Renegade demons of each demonic heritage have different ways they react to their new freedom, based on the prior sins that formed them. The following are some of the most common demon heritages to become renegades.

Babaus

Ordinary babaus in the Abyss often work as sadistic murderers or assassins, as they're typically formed from the souls of solitary killers, especially serial killers. Their sin is embodied in blood, gore, murder, and violence, granting them the colloquial name "blood demons." Renegade babaus who rejected their sin use their skills for other pursuits. Babau reversers intentionally seek to propagate the opposite of carnage, granting mercy and healing to others. Knowing anatomy in great detail in order to bring pain and suffering also provides surprising insight into medicine and healing. Meanwhile, babau renegade sinners use their substantial talent for subtlety to commit other sorts of sinful acts, such as heists or voyeurism. No matter what they choose, renegade babaus find that their skills are useful in a variety of pursuits, especially those related to battles or clandestine acts. That being said, when they take on violent professions, such as adventuring, they often struggle with the tantalizing urges of their discarded sin, urging them to continue past the point of necessity into sadism and torture. Thus, adventuring renegade babaus have to walk a difficult tightrope, as their proclivities grant them great advantages in their chosen profession, but recidivism is an occupational hazard.

Brimoraks

Abyssal brimoraks are pyromaniacs spawned from the souls of unrepentant arsonists. Their tempers and passions are as fiery as the flames they spread everywhere around them, sometimes surprising even other demons. Renegade brimoraks might have rejected the sin of arson as a predominant force, but that doesn't mean they've extinguished the flames within their souls. They typically become obsessively passionate about something else instead, pursuing that new passion with the same intensity, hot temper, and glee as they once did arson. Reverser brimoraks might become firefighters, working to put a stop to fires and save lives, using their ability to predict exactly how a fire will spread in order to combat it. Renegade sinners are able to find plenty of new ways to engage their evil impulses without setting endless fires. Other brimoraks might become passionate about less extreme pursuits, occupations, or hobbies, with interests as diverse as acting, fencing, and painting. Cooking is another favorite, but it comes with a hidden danger, as the same flame used to cook leaves a temptation to burn it all down. In the same vein, some renegade brimoraks learn how to use controlled fires to help prevent larger catastrophes in the future, channeling their love of fire for a purpose other than the wanton destruction of arson. Renegade brimoraks aren't stuck with one pursuit to replace the hole that the sin of arson left in their heart, and many of them often change their mind and become equally engaged in a new interest with mercurial pique. That doesn't mean that the joy and single-minded passion they felt toward their past pursuit wasn't real at the time, but as they learn and grow, their preferences simply change.

Dretches

Dretches drenched in their own sin are incarnations of sloth, infinitely lazy, and cowardly enough to even accept mortal summoners' commands in exchange for a reprieve from the Abyss. Thus, they are among the most likely demons to desire going renegade, but their sin of sloth makes it exceptionally difficult for them to follow through with their wish and keep on the difficult path that denying their abyssal roots requires. Reverser dretches try to be as energetic and industrious as possible, often making daily checklists, engaging friends to hold them accountable, and relying on other productivity tools to overcome their tendency to slide back into sloth. Meanwhile, renegade sinners find that pursuing other sorts of evil with gusto is enough to keep them on their own path and avoid the call of the Abyss. Sometimes a dretch goes renegade after a particularly fruitful summoning, after forming a bond with their summoner and coming to see them as a protector from the frightful violence they had waiting for them back in the Abyss. In this case, the dretch's friendship helps them to pursue a new path, as such journeys are much scarier and more perilous when traveled alone. Occasionally, a call to action or special cause galvanizes a dretch to become a renegade. When they believe in something so strongly they can't help but strive toward it with all their heart, they inherently reject sloth, even if that wasn't their original intent. In that case, their new goal or cause becomes extremely important to their worldview, and they are at risk of returning to their slothful ways if they become disillusioned or see their idealized view of their pursuit shattered before their eyes. If they overcome this hurdle, however, it allows them to continue on their path with a will that's stronger than ever before, having triumphed over one of the biggest risks to their future development. While renegade dretches have cast aside the shackles of the sin of sloth over their destiny, that doesn't mean they don't enjoy good healthy rest and relaxation. Few understand how to take it easy better than dretches, and renegades try to do so in a way that helps rejuvenate them, rather than lead them to wallow back into abject sloth.

Hezrous

Hezrous spawn from the souls of drug dealers, polluters, and similar folk who corrupted everything around them. Normally, hezrous follow suit, exuding deadly abyssal toxins that warp and twist a beautiful and pristine environment into a toxic swamp of sludge and evil. They like to indulge in simple evil pleasures, lounging around and allowing their mere presence to destroy and despoil. Renegade hezrous cast off that sin, which allows them to exist without corrupting all around them if they make an effort to do so. Reverser hezrous attempt to purify, heal, and bring forth beauty, which makes them so close to redeemed that there's a substantial overlap. Meanwhile, hezrou renegade sinners indulge in the other sorts of pleasures hezrou are known for, but while choosing to keep their environments safe from corruption, perhaps because they prefer the creature comforts of more pristine surroundings. Whatever a renegade hezrou's preferences and despite their usual focus on simple gratifications, they tend to exert a larger than life influence on their surroundings. This is why they must actively ensure that the influence doesn't despoil, lest they reactivate their surrendered sin and regain some of their metaphysical corruptive power, triggering a catastrophic spiral. Renegade hezrous rarely seek out leadership positions, since they often just want to do their own thing, but sometimes others simply follow their example or form a collective or team. This suits hezrous just fine: if everyone works together and employs their own strengths, the hezrou can enjoy their favorite things more easily. Some renegade hezrous use their connection to chemicals and transformations to become skilled alchemists or transmuters; there are many ways to make a change other than those forced upon Abyssal hezrous by the sin of corruption. After all, the difference between medicine and poison is often a matter of dosage.

Mariliths

Mariliths are among the most powerful demons who go renegade and cast off their underlying sin, and thus they have the most to lose. Nonetheless, it is paradoxically their very sin that ensures mariliths continue to do so. This is because mariliths are formed from the sin of pride, and it is that same pride that causes some mariliths to seek their own strength and to strive to forge their own path and prove their greatness. After expelling their pride and the strength it provides, mariliths are the most physically affected of any renegade demon, as their sin's power ordinarily manifests in two extra pairs of arms. A demon's body, formed of spiritual quintessence, is a reflection not of physical laws but instead of metaphysical ones, and thus, without the overwhelming sin of pride, those other arms' physicality quickly fades, leaving behind what appear to be flickering ghostly reminders, echoes in the quintessence, that appear occasionally to help the marilith out in a bind as long as they keep enough confidence about them (though not too much to sink into pride). Reverser mariliths are extremely humble and seek out potential failures as learning opportunities, to help them train to become better and better until their honest effort and humility grant them true excellence. Marilith renegade sinners believe that their clearer head without the rose-colored glasses of out of control pride makes them far better evil masterminds in whatever endeavors they choose to pursue; no more will they allow others to control them via their egos into senselessly risking their lives for little real gain leading abyssal armies. Renegade mariliths still have a commanding presence and confidence that makes them natural leaders, and their ability to predict the resulting ripples of chaos's butterfly effect make them outstanding adventuring party leaders, criminal bosses, crusaders against chaotic foes, secretly evil chancellors, and more. After a demonstration of a renegade marilith's prodigious talent, they might even be able to secure a place for themself among mortal society, which is often prejudicial to demons (if understandably so due to the fact that they are forged of pure evil).

Omoxes

Like hezrous, omoxes form from the souls of those who corrupted and polluted creatures and their surroundings in life. Abyssal omoxes lose their forms to their corruption, which once caused scholars to misclassify them as sapient Abyssal detritus rather than a true demon formed of sinful souls. However, when renegade omoxes reject their sin and the power it grants, they're able to regain something of a coherent shape. This makes it easier to interact with others, but even so, all but the most unusual omoxes still appear to be formed of sludge and waste, which has the tendency to lead others to react to their presence (and their odor) with disgust. Like hezrous, reverser omoxos try to purify and cleanse, making the world a better place, which brings them close enough to omox redeemed that they have a substantial overlap. On the other hand, omox renegade sinners delight in other sins while avoiding corrupting the world around them, like hezrou renegade sinners. To some, the image of a sapient sludge creature being mindful of polluting their environment is comical, but to renegade omoxes, this is no laughing matter. Often renegade omoxes work in groups with creatures from other ancestries or renegade demons of other heritages. This is especially helpful when it comes to cleaning up, as the omox's form sometimes means that they dirty the area around them even as they attempt to clean it. Their allies can take the final step to clean off the filth the omox left behind.

Succubi

Mortals have a fascination with carnal lust, and indeed such sins are second nature to a succubus born on the Abyss, but succubi are capable of far more varied temptations than merely those of the flesh, using lusts for power, faith, knowledge, recognition, and more to seduce mortals into sin. They represent any kind of lust taken to an extreme that twists, perverts, and destroys all around them, throwing society into chaos and twisting the local culture around their little finger. Because of various biases that exist in mortal culture towards the redemption of those who appear attractive, renegade succubi get a lot more attention than any of the other renegade demons, and they are, unfairly, more likely to earn acceptance after an unending gauntlet of proving themselves. Unfortunately, the reputation for being some of the multiverse's greatest experts in deceptively appearing to become exactly what others most want them to be means that even a truly genuinely repentant succubus is met with a great deal of skepticism. But once they are known to have cast off their sin, with enough people in society vouching for them, renegade succubi can gain an advantageous reputation far faster than most other renegade demons, a distinction that makes some of them rather uncomfortable. Renegade succubi who have cast aside the shackles of lust and temptation usually don't leave behind the positive aspects of their previous predilections; there's nothing inherently sinful about enjoying pleasure in a responsible and mutually respectful manner, for instance, and so most renegade succubi continue to do so.

Reverser succubi haven't been able to decide among themselves exactly what the best opposite of their abyssal temptation should be. Some focus on rehabilitating others, helping to pull them out of their vices and back onto the path they wanted from life as therapists, sober companions, or simply good friends. Others, however, believe that this is essentially tempting toward a more acceptable direction, and choose an ascetic lifestyle to deny themselves as many lusts as possible. Still others feel that accepting rejection themselves is the essence of rejecting the sin of lust, and so they throw themselves into situations where they're likely to be rejected over and over, teaching themselves how to turn rejection into a learning experience. The last group of reverser succubi believe that the true opposite of lust is love, and for that reason, they go on a journey trying to find what the mortal ancestries call "true love" wherever that might take them.

As for renegade sinners? Their succubus skill set makes it all too easy for them to engage in every evil deed under the sun, so as long as they can hold in their lustful urges enough to avoid completely falling back, it's easy enough for them to get by in other ways. Even so, it's much simpler to say than to do, and so they often need to join a team or support group to handle those urges, which can sometimes lead them on a long journey toward one of the other hosts.

Vrocks

As demons spawned from the sin of wrath, Abyssal vrocks typically spill forth from their chthonic home in groups to vent their rage upon other creatures, serving as shock troops for Abyssal armies or toying with prey for their own amusement. Renegade vrocks must struggle against that overwhelming anger in order to reject the sin of wrath. This isn't always easy, even for someone as strong-willed as a renegade demon, and they often need to employ anger management techniques and remind themselves to cool off when their rage hits a peak they can't control. For most renegade vrocks, the most effective techniques tend to channel that anger into something more productive, rather than simply suppress it and risk an explosion later on.

Vrock reversers go even further in trying to counter their fury, engaging in deep meditation and attempting to instill serenity in themselves and the world around them. They might even become pacifists, seeking to stop war and conflict and promote understanding and amity between people of all different nations, viewpoints, and walks of life.

On the other hand, vrock renegade sinners often rely on a variety of other sinful pleasures to rein in their destructive wrathful urges. In their view, it's hard to be too angry when you're swimming in lucre, feasts, companionship, and constant validation of your greatness. Sometimes they justify their actions as leashing their own inner wrath, with any crimes they commit to suppress their anger a smaller price to pay. After all, no one likes it when they're angry.

Regardless of their host, renegade vrocks possess a wide variety of destructive talents, but instead of using them to indulge their inner rage by inflicting pain on others, they often use them for other purposes, such as protecting what they hold dear, exploring the world on a grand adventure, or exploring their other desires for various sins. The main danger that arises when using their wrathful talents in this way is that it exposes them to situations that might make provoke their ire and make them even angrier, risking a dangerous explosion or a backslide into their sinful former demeanor. For this reason, renegade vrocks often work together with trusted companions who can watch for the telltale signs and help them calm down or take a step back before they erupt.

The Wages of Sin

While a renegade demon PC has put their sins behind them, demons are immortal fiends. With the exception of a rare few renegades, who rebelled from their sinful shackles nearly the moment they formed as demons, most demons have lived for many years in their old lives before their transition into a renegade. With a life as a demon comes a history, often a truly gruesome and horrific one. Depending on what sorts of stories you and your group want to tell with the renegade demon character, it's a fairly common trope for the wages of sin to come to roost as their past comes back to haunt them. For a formerly evil human who wants to be redeemed, a troubling past might include a few specific evil deeds they regret, but a demon PC might have thousands (or, for a truly ancient demon, millions) of atrocities to their name. For this reason, the demon player and GM should discuss the extent to which they want to bring their character's past into the story. It would also be worthwhile to check with the other players' comfort levels before delving into specifically gruesome sins of the past, as these topics could touch upon areas of discomfort for them, doubly so in that they know the demon character was once the person who committed these atrocities and is now in their party.

Alignment and Religion

Renegade demons can have any alignment, but they tend to have an extremely powerful and meaningful relationship with the good and evil axis of their alignment, whatever their alignment. If they're neutral or even good, they struggled mightily to reach that point, and if they're still evil, they still must have had a good reason to abandon so much demonic power in order to carve their own path. Renegade demons are likely to retain the chaotic alignment shared with ordinary demons, but neutral and even lawful renegade demons exist as well. Renegade demons usually worship any deities except demon lords, as even those who have remained chaotic evil have turned their backs on their Abyssal pasts.
