Cruel and Unusual Punishment.name: 残酷及非常规惩罚 Cruel and Unusual Punishment
Cruel and Unusual Punishment.text:
出自艾瑟里娅·尼克斯的档案
残酷且非同寻常的惩罚

粗野。非人。怪物。

我们用来描述他人、将他们妖魔化的词语，映照出我们自身的偏见。非人又有什么错？成为怪物意味着什么？也许，那些心怀对他人恶意的人才是真正的怪物？但请原谅我的迂腐；你想听的是恶鬼。的确，在这多元宇宙所能提供的一切经历中，这些灵魂遭遇了最不公正的那些。曾经，他们是神使，被赋予守护一处守护结界的职责。然后，他们常常并非出于自身过错，却失败了。

为此，他们被施以少数几种明显比彻底湮灭还要更糟的命运之一：被困住，被迫只能勉强感知周遭世界，却无法进行哪怕最微小的互动，也无法体验简单的喜悦。神祇该为滥用权能、夺走这些温和灵魂的自主而感到羞耻；也该为责怪一个往往才是受害者的人而感到羞耻。这些受罚的灵体在那时还不是恶鬼，只是失去肉身的神使；但若他们反抗不公的惩罚，与唯一愿意帮助他们的存在达成交易，并为自己创造一个物质形体以重新获得自主，不知怎地这就让他们成了怪物。不可饶恕。恶魔。

而事实上，他们当中的许多，因被迫忍受毫无缘由的折磨而形成了扭曲的视角。他们是神祇造就的恶魔。但其中也有一些恶鬼仍然仁善，甚至英勇。这确实很稀有度，而且他们必须拒绝那些帮助他们逃离残酷命运、却又希望腐化他们的邪恶势力所抛出的诱人力量。可这更进一步证明了神祇所作所为之错误。这些离经叛道的恶鬼人数稀少、饱受误解，但这正使他们的故事尤其值得被讲述！

——艾瑟里娅·尼克斯，空间元素先驱，怪物辩护者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Cruel and Unusual Punishment

Brutes. Inhuman. Monsters.

The words we use to describe others, to demonize them, reflect our own prejudices. What’s wrong with being inhuman? What does it mean to be a monster? Perhaps those who hold wickedness in their hearts for others are the true monsters? But forgive my pedantry; you wanted to hear about oni. Truly, these souls have had among the most unfair experiences the multiverse has to offer. Once, they were kami, charged with guarding a ward. And then, often through no fault of their own, they failed.

For this, they were punished with one of the few fates clearly worse than even oblivion: being trapped, forced to be able to barely perceive the world around them but unable to so much as interact or experience the simple joys. Shame on the gods for abusing their power to steal the agency of these gentle souls, for blaming someone who is so often the victim. These punished spirits aren’t oni yet at that point, just disembodied kami, but if they rebel against their unjust punishment, make a deal with the only entities who will help them, and create a physical form for themselves to gain agency again, somehow that makes them monsters. Unforgivable. Fiends.

And in truth, many of them have a twisted perspective based on the torture they were forced to endure for little rhyme or reason. They are the fiends the gods made. But some of these oni are still benevolent, even heroic. It’s a rarity, yes, and it requires them to decline a tempting offer of power from the evil forces who helped them escape their cruel fate, hoping to corrupt them. But it’s yet more proof that what the gods have done is wrong. These oni mavericks are few and misunderstood, but that makes their stories especially worth telling!

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Oni Ancestry.name: 恶鬼族裔 Oni Ancestry
Oni Ancestry.text:
Oni arise when a kami fails to defend their ward. Stories of oni sometimes focus on cases where the kami was negligent or even openly complicit in the failure, but the truth is that in many cases, that failure only occurred due to circumstances outside their control. A guardian isn’t absolute, no matter how hard they try. Regardless of their culpability, these kami receive an incomprehensibly cruel torture, being forced to watch a world they can never experience again. Whether right away or only after a lengthy period of torment, many eventually take the helping hand offered to them by the forces of evil, allowing them to return to the world as oni.

The process of an oni’s incarnation into the mortal world is complex, even for the deity-level evil entities who enact such a scheme, and so they employ several methods to make the transition easier. For one, each oni crystallizes around the concept of a particular type of humanoid already present in the world, and that target makes the process of transmigration much simpler and more reliable. Perhaps most crucially to the evil patron’s risk assessment, the process of creating a new oni from a punished kami has two steps. First, the patron incarnates the oni’s spirit into a new physical body on the Material Plane, and only afterward can the pact be finalized. Deities of evil would prefer otherwise, but the trouble arises from the fact that it’s difficult to quantify the elusive insubstantial beings that the exiled kami are; the same punishment that prevented them from interacting with the mortal world also makes it essentially impossible to pin them down to a magical contract.

As a result, it’s possible for an oni to accept the incarnation and then renege on any agreement they might have made or change their mind even if they initially intended to comply. Because of this, would-be patrons withhold a large part of the power they’ve offered until the second phase, where they enact their evil pact and empower the oni with vile energies, making the oni an instant powerhouse in service of the forces of iniquity. That means that oni mavericks, those oni who back out of the bargain or refuse to become evil or follow their vile savior’s orders, begin their new lives as oni significantly weaker than their malevolent and subservient counterparts. However, their strong independent streak and clear vision for their new life in the mortal world allow them to quickly garner strength in their own way. And that’s a good thing since they’ve made a new enemy in their spurned former patron, and who knows what will happen to them after they die without that patron’s help!

If you want to play a character punished harshly and unjustly (or perhaps justly) for failure, who experienced great loss as a result, and who is seeking a second life on your own terms, you should play an oni.

You Might...

  • Cherish certain small joys that you had been denied before you incarnated as an oni.
  • Bristle at unjust, unfair, or overly harsh punishments, whether they’re imposed on you or others.
  • Struggle with the consequences of denying a powerful evil entity.

Others Probably...

  • Can’t tell the difference between you and other oni, or even other fiends, if they are ill-informed.
  • Don’t understand why you take so much joy in the world around you.
  • Find your enthusiasm and zest for life invigorating.
  • Physical Description

Oni vary in appearance, with each heritage sharing certain features with a different humanoid ancestry. For instance, onidoshi closely resemble ogres, while fire yai have some similarities to fire giants. Oni of most heritages tend to have a rich coloration and horns of varying size, and all oni of yai heritages have a striking third eye at the center of their forehead, which acts as a source for some of their mystical potential. Oni mavericks occasionally have physical features that look significantly different from other oni, often because they lack a key feature common to other oni of their heritage, such as horns or a third eye. Water yai mavericks, in particular, consistently don’t grow to the same size as other water yai.

Physical Description

Oni vary in appearance, with each heritage sharing certain features with a different humanoid ancestry. For instance, onidoshi closely resemble ogres, while fire yai have some similarities to fire giants. Oni of most heritages tend to have a rich coloration and horns of varying size, and all oni of yai heritages have a striking third eye at the center of their forehead, which acts as a source for some of their mystical potential. Oni mavericks occasionally have physical features that look different from other oni, often because they lack a key feature common to other oni of their heritage, such as horns or a third eye. Water yai and wind yai mavericks, in particular, don't grow to the same size as normal.

Earth yai, oni whose form is based on stone giants, possess rocky bodies with rugged and jagged appearances, alongside a third eye in the center of their forehead like other yai.

Fire yai, oni with a physical form based on fire giants, have skin as red as fire with a third eye in the center of their forehead and a large stature, like other yai. Their facial features and horn positioning tend to give their faces a passing resemblance to a demonic mask, at least when seen in a still image. However, the dynamism of their expressions ensures that no one who watches a fire yai speak could ever mistake their expression for a mere mask.

Ice yai, oni whose form is based on those of frost giants, have deep blue skin with sharp teeth, particularly a pair of tusks on the lower jaw, and long nails. Like other yai, they have a third eye in the center of their forehead and are as tall as a giant. Ice yai have hair and horns that are seemingly made up entirely of ice. Because many ice yai are training junkies, ice yai of all genders tend to have extremely impressive toned muscular physiques.

Ja noi more closely resemble hobgoblins than most other heritages do the ancestry on which their form was based, except they have rich red-brown skin instead of the typical hobgoblin skin tones and a prominent brow ridge where other oni usually have horns. Their eyes are typically pure black, and stand slightly taller on average than hobgoblins.

Kuwa, oni with appearances based on humans, have golden skin that seems almost metallic in the right lighting, with hair of varying colors offering a sharp contrast to their body's hues. They possess horns, sharp teeth, and claw-like hands. Their most distinctive feature is their huge bulging eyes, which tend to give them a perpetually intense-looking expression.

Nogitsune, oni whose form is based on kitsune, have vulpine features and a compelling, though predatorial bearing, with a fox head, pronounced canines, and a mass of bushy tails.

Onidoshi, oni whose features are based on ogres and who are sometimes known as "ogre mages," are the stereotypical image that those who know of oni usually think of when they hear the word. They have large, hulking bodies with horns, pointed ears, and tusks. While most other oni heritages have skin tones that center around shades of the same color, onidoshi have a startlingly wide variety of colorations, which are vibrant no matter the color. The two most common skin colors are red and blue. In stories about onidoshi, it is often popular among other ancestries to present two oni friends, rivals, or siblings, one red and one blue, with contrasting personality traits, though onidoshi quickly tire of such attempts to stereotype them based on their appearance.

Water yai, oni who patterned their form in the mortal world after storm giants, are even larger than fire and ice yai, though water yai mavericks aren't quite as immense. They have aqua skin the color of water, though it sometimes ranges even paler than that, with dark hair framing the third eye all yai possess. Like many oni, they have pointed teeth and long nails, though their horns tend to be somewhat smaller and less prominent than many other oni, possibly obscured by their hair, depending on their hairstyle. Due to their fondness for the objects of the mortal world, water yai bind a watery raiment to themselves, a possession that can never be taken away even should the unjust gods try to punish them again. This raiment traditionally takes the form of a kimono, but depending on the water yai, it might resemble a different garb.

Wind yai, oni whose form is based on cloud giants, tend to wear ostentatious robes to showcase their control over their appearance, surrounded by an almost metaphysical edgy gloom. They have a third eye in the center of their forehead like other yai.

Society

Even though each oni arises in the world through a cruel punishment and a malevolent pact, that doesn't mean they're vanishingly rare, as the entities that offer their bargains to disembodied former kami tend to create more oni than people might expect (which raises the question of just how many kami have been sentenced to horrific punishment). This, in turn, leads to oni occasionally banding together to form oni societies, though in many cases, they prefer joining a society of the ancestry whose form matches their heritage, all the better to be a big fish in a small pond. Oni mavericks are even more likely to go their own way compared to their counterparts who complete the contract with evil. Even so, oni mavericks recognize the value in having some sort of support network in case their would-be patron seeks revenge, and so they have formed five factions, sometimes called habatsu, allowing like-minded mavericks to connect and potentially offer assistance, should they so choose. Because mavericks have different tendencies based on their heritage, some of the factions have more or fewer members of certain heritages.

The following are descriptions for the nine oni heritages presented in this book, followed by the five factions.

Heritages

An oni's heritage depends on the original kami's nature and yearning for the material world, and it determines which mortal ancestry their new form resembles most.

Fire Yai

Fire yai are typically fans of warfare and battle, studying strategies, tactics, and maneuvers. They can easily become swept up in the thrill of battle, which can occasionally cause them to get carried away during a fight. For many fire yai, the life and death struggle of battle was one of the realities of the mortal world that felt the most lacking when they lost their connection and were forced to drift through the world as disembodied spirits. That said, fire yai mavericks sometimes buck these trends, and not all of them are as hot-blooded as other fire yai. Many mavericks retain these trends but put them to use in service of other causes. For instance, fire yai saviors channel their desire for battle toward liberating others from unfair fates. Fire yai mavericks are relatively evenly distributed among the five factions, though they are somewhat less likely to be hedonists than the other four. They tend to be lawful more often than chaotic, though only slightly, as they prefer a disciplined approach to control their fiery passion for battle.

Earth Yai

To an earth yai, the ultimate horror of their discorporate punishment was the way they were made to feel powerless, victims within their own consciousness unable to act upon the horrors they could still perceive within the world around them. As such, they become fixated on the pursuit of power; not because they necessarily wish to exercise that power, but because they need to ultimately have that power in their pocket as an ace in the hole, ready to deploy if the need arises. This leads earth yai to seek out great power by any means, especially when that power is capable of acting as a deterrent or a motivator without the need to use it. This same power allows compassionate earth yai maverick to protect what's dear to them and tyrannical earth yai to seize control. Unlike ice yai, who are focused on perfecting their own body, earth yai don't mind if the source of their power comes from physical strength, mystic study, the cooperation of allies, or any other vector: whatever provides the strongest impact is best. Earth yai mavericks wind up in all five factions relatively evenly, but whichever faction they join, they tend to adopt a larger-than-life role with their obsession with the pursuit of power. Earth yai atoners right all the wrongs, while earth yai hedonists pursue all the pleasures. Earth yai don't tend to have any particular alignment tendencies; while they're united in their commitment to never be powerless again, they're divided on how to use that power.

Ice Yai

Ice yai tend to be obsessed with physical perfection, constantly training themselves, pushing their bodies to their limits, and mastering all sorts of martial techniques. Losing their corporeal form and ability to affect the world was a traumatizing event for the former kami who would one day become ice yai, and in response, now that they have a body of their own, most of them cherish their new form, spending much of their effort to sculpt it as they choose. Additionally, they tend to prefer exerting soft power to garner influence rather than default to violent means like many other oni, especially fire yai and ja noi. Ice yai mavericks have a wider variety of opinions on the matter than other ice yai, but the tendency remains. Ice yai are somewhat more likely to become codifiers than any other faction, though they are represented in all five oni maverick factions. Despite their self-discipline, ice yai paradoxically tend to have a somewhat more chaotic outlook on life in general than lawful, which also makes ice yai codifiers more chaotic than the rest of the faction on average.

Ja Noi

Much like fire yai, ja noi can be overcome with their passion for battle, though fire yai are somewhat more passionate about large-scale warfare, while ja noi focus more on individual battles and the blood of the kill. That said, both fire yai and ja noi appreciate both types of combat, as they both became unmoored from the life or death struggles of the mortal world, and as such, both types of oni are often willing to work together to achieve their aims. Those ja noi who fully accept the fiendish pact tend to fit in perfectly with hobgoblin society. While other types of oni might hide their true nature to infiltrate humanoid society, hobgoblins look up to and respect ja noi for their strength, combat skills, and commanding presence, and so they are happy to promote them to positions of leadership. Ja noi mavericks, on the other hand, are much less likely to fit into the rigid militarism of hobgoblin society for the same reason they chose to become a maverick. Ja noi mavericks are distributed among the five factions even more evenly than fire yai, due to their comparatively higher number of hedonists. Some ja noi mavericks cultivate strong connections, usually rivalries, but sometimes friendships with other ja noi mavericks from different factions. This interaction between factions leads to a greater ability to collaborate and coordinate, and ironically, the ja noi maverick gossip and information network is stronger than several other types of oni maverick who are usually more loquacious. Ja noi mavericks are more likely to be lawful than chaotic, as many of them craved structure and form during their time adrift without a body.

Kuwa

Kuwa are oni whose primary despair from their cruel, godly punishment was the isolation imposed upon them as bodiless undetectable spirits unable to interact with others. As such, they crave companionship and society, hoping to find an unobtrusive and quietly powerful position in a community and then use it to live a comfortable life filled with the finer things. Perhaps because of the way they were burned back when they had responsibility for their ward as kami, kuwa prefer to avoid prominent positions that put them officially in charge of something. Instead, they often hide in the shadows of another leader, trying to find a way to exercise soft or subtle power in order to achieve their goals. Kuwa often disguise their oni nature, as that makes it easier to avoid standing out, and they prefer that if possible. Kuwa mavericks are sometimes more willing to reveal themselves to their friends and allies, though even mavericks might prefer to remain secretive in order to more easily blend in as part of the ingroup. Kuwa who stick to the shadows tend to be more likely to join the free spirit or hedonist factions, while kuwa who reveal their oni nature might become codifiers or saviors. Because of the way they relate to hierarchies and social structures, kuwa are more likely to be lawful than chaotic.

Nogitsune

When a disembodied former kami craves not companionship, like those destined to become kuwa, but the thrill of social influence and the ability to elicit emotional reactions from others, they become nogitsune, with vulpine forms resembling kitsune. Nogitsune find places in society where they can influence others most easily, and they typically aren't particular about how they achieve that influence. Some nogitsune play pranks, while others use silver tongues to spin grifts and scams, while still others build up social influence as leaders of social movements or cults of personality. Ultimately, while nogitsune seek to surround themselves with other people to experience their reactions, most are solitary by nature when it comes to their inner circle, fearing intimacy as a result of their trauma from their horrific punishment. This might seem like a paradox to others, but nogitsune understand what it means to be truly alone in the center of a crowd, even as they bask in the spotlight. These lone wolf (or perhaps lone fox) tendencies lead nogitsune to be reticent to join alliances of equals, such as adventuring parties. When they do, they sometimes consider their role within such groups as slightly more distant than the other members, influencing the others toward the nogitsune's preferred outcome rather than coming to a shared decision via mutual communication and understanding. Those who've learned how to slowly and carefully grow closer to a nogitsune understand this dichotomy and accept that the process might be gradual, often with one step forward and two steps back. Nogitsune mavericks are most often free spirits or hedonists, though they have no particularly tendencies when it comes to alignment. They often disguise as kitsune to better manipulate others and hide their true inner feelings. They sometimes take this obfuscation to the next level by disguising as a kitsune who is disguising in a tailless humanoid form; that way, if someone penetrates one level of the disguise, they might be satisfied to discover the "truth" of the kitsune identity.

Onidoshi

Onidoshi arise from punished kami whose greatest sorrow during their long and disproportionate torture was the lack of agency and control over anything around them. All they could do was watch as things happened, often including horrific things they couldn't countenance, and there was absolutely nothing they could do to prevent or mitigate these disasters. For this reason, onidoshi seek to take control of the environment around them. Onidoshi, who take evil pacts, channel this need for control by often becoming fiendish bullies and performing such cliched acts of villainy as holding villages hostage and demanding tribute. Onidoshi mavericks find therapeutic release over their loss of control in different ways, which are often more productive or at least less destructive. This leads them to become free spirits if they want to regain their agency by seizing their own destiny, atoners if they feel that amending their original mistake is part of taking back their own destiny, or saviors if they want to make sure that what happens to them never happens to anyone else. Onidoshi tend to be lawful more often than chaotic, as they find a defined plan to regain their own agency (though rarely to the extent that they submit their own free will to a grand plan, meaning they don't fit in as well with codifiers). This also makes onidoshi who join the free spirit faction to retain the maximum amount of control over their own life more lawful than their fellow faction members.

Water Yai

Water yai are oni who missed their material form and the material objects that make up the mortal world when they were forced to become disembodied spirits with no one and nothing that belonged to them or could even see them. As such, now that they have become oni, they often collect souvenirs, mementos, and knickknacks, allowing them to help commemorate their experiences and prove that they exist. This desperate materialism leads others to view water yai as blatantly greedy, and indeed, evil water yai who complete their contracts often become incredibly greedy as well. On the other hand, some water yai mavericks blend their materialism with compassion and nostalgia; their care and wonder when it comes to their possessions is genuine, and it leads to them being more likely to awaken objects into tsukumogami, wished alive poppets, or other forms of creatures formed when a well-loved object gains sapience and a spark of life. Water yais' connection to the material world manifests as a physical outfit made entirely out of water, often styled as a kimono, which they use to swaddle themselves and ensure they will never be without possessions ever again. Water yai mavericks tend to be hedonists over other factions, as they focus on gathering new things, though there are plenty of water yai from the other factions as well. They are often chaotic rather than lawful, allowing sentiment and emotion to guide their path rather than a strict set of principles.

Wind Yai

Wind yais' greatest anguish during their torturous punishment was their lack of control over themselves and the chaotic world around them. This feeling of powerlessness led many of the fallen kami who would become wind yai to adopt a gloomy and fatalistic manner that followed them into their oni form, leading to many wind yai acting as inveterate edgelords, surrounded by an almost palpable aura of gloom and speaking in over-the-top fashion about feeling a "dark wind of despair" or an "air of bleak devastation." At the same time, wind yai seek to tame the untamable, causing them to quickly become control freaks once they've adopted their new oni form. Even the chaotic whimsy of the breeze is something most wind yai seek to predict and control, and some particularly extreme wind yai can become peeved when a stray zephyr blows in a way counter to their preference. Wind yai mavericks often become codifiers above all the other factions, as no other faction captures their desire to systematically seize back their lost agency via control of their environment. For much the same reason, wind yai are significantly more likely to be lawful than they are to be chaotic. Because music serves as a mathematical way to channel the chaos of sound into a controlled, structured medium, wind yai are especially likely to become bards if they have any musical talent, or to seek to travel alongside a bard if they don't.

Factions

The following factions allow oni mavericks to support each other when the situation demands it.

Atoners

As a general rule, oni are the fallen kami who have come to realize that their punishment from the gods was unfair or at least disproportionate. Otherwise, why would they have made an agreement with evil forces in the first place, even if they reneged and became mavericks. However, for atoners, one of the five factions of oni mavericks, the question isn’t so clear-cut. Among the ranks of exiled kami, situations vary wildly, from those who were extremely negligent with their wards and might as well have caused the calamity that led to their punishment to those who performed above and beyond any possible expectation but failed anyway and were punished exactly the same as all other failures. Atoners are oni mavericks who feel guilty about their failure and want to try to make up for it somehow.

Outsiders sometimes expect the atoner faction to be filled with oni mavericks who definitely made a mistake that led to their ward’s destruction. However, the faction is evenly split between those atoners and others who were blameless but are extremely anxious with a strong sense of responsibility and culpability that flared up to a pathological level thanks to the gods’ cruel punishment.

In most cases, an atoner’s former ward is irretrievably gone, and there is no way to directly put it right. In the few situations where that’s possible, the atoner likely works to do just that. However, in the vast majority of cases, the atoner chooses a new path forward that is meant to atone for a blemish they can never truly remove. They might fight to prevent future disasters of the sort that destroyed their ward, protect other similar wards, seek revenge against the type of evil creature that devastated their ward, or come up with other creative ways to find absolution within their own hearts.

Most atoners know that the cruel and unforgiving deities in charge of kami will never forgive them no matter what they do; they don’t delude themselves otherwise. And yet they were willing to make a (sometimes literal) deal with the devil in order to make things right. This level of willpower and resolve are indicative of an oni maverick with an extremely strong intrinsic sense of responsibility or justice. Thus, atoners tend to be good or lawful, and sometimes both. Only a tiny handful of the faction’s members are evil, as the evil perspective on being punished unjustly is rarely to try to make things right. Those few evil atoners are mostly lawful evil, and they didn’t always start evil during their time as oni mavericks; sometimes, they began with good intentions, but they found that in pursuing their atonement, they strayed farther and farther into harsh justice, uncaring control, or revenge. For instance, one evil atoner wanted to protect an endangered medicinal plant and its surrounding woodlands from deforestation and overharvesting while avoiding killing the local villagers, but the only solution they could reach was to seize control of the villages to make a small fiefdom and establish environmentally friendly laws, mind-controlling anyone in the fiefdom who wouldn’t fall in line.

Oni Adventurers

Oni maverick adventurers choose classes that allow them to indulge in their interests and passions, which can vary based on the maverick’s heritage and faction. For instance, saviors often become liberator champions, ice yai prefer to be monks, and fire yai and ja noi prefer martial classes like fighter and barbarian. Oni mavericks share an original background as a fallen kami, but they sometimes start in a non-adventuring career when they first become oni, only to later to become adventurers when their ideals change or their funds require it. These backgrounds tend to be those allowing them to indulge themselves in the material world around them, including artist, bandit, barkeep, bookkeeper, charlatan, cook, criminal, entertainer, false medium, fireworks performer, gambler, gladiator, magical merchant, martial disciple, merchant, noble, tax collector, or warrior.

Codifiers

When a fallen kami becomes an oni maverick, turning their back on the evil forces that offered them a new life as oni, some attempt to create a new system to replace the cruel and unfair punishments forced upon them in the past, or at least to show others how much better their way can be. These oni mavericks join the codifier faction, a collection of mavericks who share this lofty goal. While codifiers all agree that something has to change and that the ways of the deities who preside over kami are unjust and inhumane, few codifiers can agree on what this new system should be. Every codifier has their own pet theories of fairness, morality, ethics, philosophy, and governance, and there’s nothing they love more than debating with each other, or with anyone else who is willing to listen, as to why their new system is best and should be adopted to replace the current status quo. In fact, one of the easiest ways to befriend a codifier is to show interest in their proposed system, as long as the potential new friend is willing to listen patiently for hours, offering occasional questions or comments to show that they are paying attention and understanding the rationales behind the codifier’s proposals. Codifiers as a whole are willing to compromise and change their mind after a spirited debate, as they feel the inflexibility of the old system is one of its biggest flaws. As such, they often adjust their new system, tweaking and tinkering with parameters in keeping with new experiences, debates, and discussions that show them a potential flaw or corner case in their current thinking.

While it’s true that the desire to codify a new system for all kami makes codifiers more likely to be lawful than chaotic, it’s not a universal tendency. Sometimes, chaotic oni mavericks desire to replace the status quo because they feel the current system is too rigid and antiquated; such chaotic codifiers have innovative ideas for new paths forward, and they often flit between various modifications much faster and more whimsically than other codifiers.

Oni Settlements

While oni mavericks are rare, the factions have set up a few gathering places where they can meet, discuss, and debate each other and the members of other factions. This also provides them a degree of protection should the forces of the evil deities they spurned come calling. Even so, most oni mavericks live in settlements with other members of their associated humanoid ancestry or heritage. Their ability to disguise themselves with magic allows them to blend in seamlessly and earn positions of trust and respect.

Free Spirits

Often opposed to codifiers, free spirits are oni mavericks who are sick of allowing others to tell them what to do. After being punished unfairly and harshly by the kami deities, free spirits won’t allow anyone to be in charge of their future ever again, not even the evil entity who allowed them to escape their eternity of horrific, lonely torment. It’s not that they weren’t grateful for the assistance, but their experiences have led them to trust issues with authority or a strong aversion to allowing others to decide their fate. When a free spirit hears a codifier rambling about their new system for kami, the free spirit typically reacts in disgust, opining that even an improved system is still a fetter, a chain to bind them down and bring them harm.

Most free spirits love exploring the world and everything around them, having felt how much they were missing during their long and agonizing punishment. Even those not interested in literal exploration or travel might have other aspects of themselves or their surroundings that they wish to explore. Still, others have little desire to see anything new but instead seek to remain free so that they can surround themselves with their favorite comforts time and again. However, this latter approach overlaps enough with the hedonist faction that such mavericks sometimes become hedonists instead. This conceptual similarity leads free spirits to ally with hedonists on occasion when the factions quarrel with each other, as both factions place personal desires above any particular higher calling.

Free spirits are much more likely to be chaotic than lawful, a fact which comes as little surprise to anyone with even a passing familiarity with the faction and their principles. The lawful free spirits, few and far between though they might be, are still extremely individualistic and independent compared to other lawful characters; they might simply believe in a more lawful approach on a smaller scale.

Failure and Punishment

When playing an oni maverick character, it’s worth spending some time to consider how your character initially failed the cruel and unfair deities who presided over kami and their wards. Was your failure an intentional and negligent choice, a tragic mistake, or the work of an unbeatable opponent? Exactly how did you fail, and what happened to your ward and the people and places around it? When you were being punished, forced to live as an insubstantial being who couldn’t influence the world or communicate with anyone, what parts of the mortal world did you miss most? What was stolen from you that caused you the most pain? Now that you can experience those precious things again, what does it mean to you, and how have you approached it? By answering these questions, you can start to understand your oni maverick character’s feelings and motivations.

Hedonists

Hedonists are the oni mavericks who behave most similarly to other oni, at least on a surface level. After returning from a hellish torment without agency or connections, they fill their lives with pleasures and indulgences to try to help themselves find happiness. The main thing that separates them from oni who accepted the contract with the evil entity that freed them from their torture is the fact that they reneged on the deal. Their reasons for backing out might have been at least vaguely moral; perhaps they wanted to indulge in luxury but didn’t want to harm others to do so. On the other hand, they might simply have had no compunctions to betray anyone, looking out only for themselves while refusing to allow their evil patron to ruin their fun. Pactsworn malevolent oni treat hedonists worse than even the other factions of mavericks, as they view hedonists as traitors who rebelled simply as a fit of pique or because, like the scorpion from the fable with the frog, it was their nature to do so.

From their own perspective, hedonists aren’t evil or good. They just want to have fun, to enjoy life, and to help make up for the terrible things they’ve experienced. Not forget them; they know that’s impossible, and it’s part of what made them what they are. But to slowly heal and move past their trauma by treating themselves. Since hedonists are less likely to use malicious schemes to gain the necessary funds for their lifestyle than pactsworn oni are, they often need to find a more humane way to get rich quickly. Surprisingly enough, adventuring tends to be a good option, as oni possess power and a unique perspective on the world that allow them to succeed as adventurers, and adventuring parties sometimes haul back more treasure in a day than someone with a job could earn in income in a month. Most hedonist adventurers try to go on a single adventure with a big payday and then live the high life off the proceeds until they’re forced to set out again for more funds. While their motives aren’t pure, hedonists are surprisingly great companions for adventurers who aren’t too judgmental about their goals. After all, hedonists want the mission to succeed quickly and efficiently with minimum danger, harm, or fuss so that they can go back to living in the lap of luxury. What’s more, hedonists tend to miss the companionship they were denied during their punishment as formless spirits whose voices reached no one, meaning they try their best to get along with their fellow adventurers in order to stave off loneliness. This affection toward their comrades continues after the adventure ends, and so a hedonist is likely to invite their whole adventuring party to live it large with them between missions, throwing huge parties, acquiring the most delicious food, and hiring the best entertainment.

Hedonists sometimes have a bad reputation, and while their self-centered philosophy means they’re somewhat less likely to be good than some of the other factions of oni mavericks, it doesn’t actually mean they’re incredibly likely to be evil. Good hedonists tend to focus on helping others join in a happy life while building friendships, romance, and companionship to the point that helping others becomes the true end rather than simply a means to an end for them.

Saviors

All oni mavericks respond to their cruel and unusual punishment differently, but members of the savior faction have perhaps the most altruistic reaction: after experiencing such injustice, they vow never to leave anyone else to helplessly face such a fate alone. Wherever there are corrupt magistrates issuing unjust punishments, cruel lords taxing the peasantry to the point they can’t put food on the table, or other villains who use the system to crush the weak and powerless under their heel, saviors act to champion the cause of the powerless, give a voice to the voiceless, or at least provide leverage to victims who had none they could use against their oppressors.

Saviors and atoners find a lot of common ground in the way they both seek to perform acts of benevolence, and so the two factions get along well, and there are even a few oni mavericks who belong to both, hoping to save others from fates like their own as part of their atonement for their own failures. When the factions bicker, their bloc finds themselves up against free spirits and hedonists, with the codifier faction breaking the stalemate. As is apparent from their predilections, saviors often seek to become adventurers or heroes, the better to become the change they want to see. They often sign up with groups that share their cause and can thus make strong common ground with righteous allies such as silver dragons if their ideals align… at least as long as their potential allies are willing to give an oni ally a chance.

Since they seek to protect others from corrupt or unfair systems and the people who exploit those systems to their own advantage, saviors tend to be chaotic good, and they’re especially fond of the liberator class for the same. Saviors are almost never evil, and those who cross that line, performing atrocities to overthrow order in the name of helping the helpless, are typically denounced by the faction once their sins come to light. These fallen saviors might become the very thing they despised, especially if being ostracized by their faction leads them to return to the only being who never abandoned them: the evil deity who reached out and offered them a hand the last time they lost everything.

Alignment and Religion

Oni mavericks can have any alignment, though they’re somewhat less likely to be evil since, in most cases, if they were willing to accept evil, they wouldn’t have needed to renege on their contract and become mavericks in the first place. Whether they tend toward lawful alignment, chaotic alignment, or neither depends on their heritage and faction.

Oni mavericks worship a wide variety of deities with only two exceptions. First and most absolutely, they explicitly broke faith with the deities who helped create oni, so they can never worship those deities. Second, oni mavericks tend to despise the deities who rule over the punishments for kami, as almost all of them feel the punishments are disproportionate and unfair, except perhaps for a few atoners. Their choice of deities depends greatly on their faction. For instance, hedonists enjoy deities of indulgence, feasting, and parties, such as Amon or Ebrugeses on Alacar, while codifiers prefer deities of order, such as Tovah or Castili on Alacar, and free spirits tend to worship deities of freedom, like Lilin on Alacar.

Names

Oni mavericks often keep the original names they had as kami, but they also sometimes choose a name for themselves that they feel represents their new self better than the original did. Either way, they tend to be single names without a surname.

Sample Names

Akinaru, Enma, Fujin, Goki, Momiji, Nezuko, Ongyoki, Otakemaru, Raijin, Rem, Shuna, Shuten-douji, Tarakudo, Urasue, Zenki.

Oni Heritages

Each oni heritage has unique abilities based around the form of a specific humanoid creature. Choose one of the following oni heritages at 1st level, which each includes an associated humanoid form, language, and size. If you choose a different heritage without a listed humanoid form, language, and size, such as a versatile heritage, your oni is Medium, your associated humanoid is the most common Medium humanoid ancestry prevalent where you grew up, and the language is one associated with that humanoid (or the most common regional language, if the language associated with that humanoid is Common).

Earth Yai

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.z5ieOFd0ffiPymmV]{Earth Yai}

You're an earth yai, an oni whose form is patterned after a stone giant who typically strives to increase your power and ability to exert your will. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is stone giant, and your language is Jotun. You gain the earth and giant traits. You ignore difficult terrain from rubble and rock, and greater difficult terrain from rubble and rock is only difficult terrain for you. You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.beyw5bdA5hkQbmaG]{Terrain Stalker} skill feat for rubble, even if you don't meet the feat's prerequisites.

Fire Yai

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.e70Mzwk1QdokFFJd]{Fire yai}

You're a fire yai, an oni whose form is patterned after a fire giant who typically live for battles, strategy, and war. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is fire giant, and your language is Jotun. You gain the fire and giant traits. You gain resistance to fire equal to half your level (minimum 1), and you treat environmental heat effects as if they were one step less extreme (incredible heat becomes extreme, extreme heat becomes severe, and so on).

Ice Yai

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.LWVU7q14JQSwf2tT]{Ice Yai}

You're an ice yai, an oni whose form is patterned after a frost giant who typically strive to perfect their physical form. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is frost giant, and your language is Jotun. You gain the cold and giant traits. You gain resistance to cold equal to half your level (minimum 1), and you treat environmental cold effects as if they were one step less extreme (incredible cold becomes extreme, extreme cold becomes severe, and so on).

Ja Noi

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.BcCegHCaOzwQfFxF]{Ja Noi}

You're a ja noi, an oni whose form is patterned after a hobgoblin and who typically crave one on one battles and the blood of the kill. Your size is Medium, your associated humanoid is hobgoblin, and your language is Goblin. You gain the goblin trait. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-actions.Item.6roxOmKgdH55P518]{Serene Discipline} free action.

Kuwa

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.Er9znFcThbfV25Zb]{Kuwa}

You're a kuwa, an oni whose form is patterned after a human and who typically desire companionship and a place in society. Your size is Medium, your associated humanoid is human, and your language is a local human regional language. You gain the human trait. You're trained in Diplomacy, and you gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.4RjDxgvNXNl5GG9d]{Hobnobber} skill feat.

Nogitsune

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.EKFkAgAIjtMz9M1e]{Nogitsune}

You're a nogitsune, an oni whose form is patterned after a kitsune and who typically desire to influence or manipulate others. Your size is Medium, your associated humanoid is kitsune, and you can additionally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.ancestryfeatures.Item.Ymg6WqeJqOyLJLEr]{Change Shape (Kitsune)} into a tailless form, which is a common Medium humanoid ancestry prevalent where you grew up (typically human). This form is the same age and body type as your kitsune alternate form and has roughly analogous physical traits, such as hair color. Your language is the language of your tailless form (a local human regional language for a human tailless form). You gain the kitsune trait. You're trained in Deception, and you gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.B6HbYsLBWb1RR6Fx]{Charming Liar} skill feat.

Onidoshi

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.RylDOgFkmZ6y36Zt]{Onidoshi}

You're an onidoshi, an oni whose form is patterned after an ogre who typically desire agency and control of their situation. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is ogre, and your language is Jotun. You gain the giant trait. You're trained in Intimidation, and you gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.ar2DUlvDK4LDcH9J]{Quick Coercion} skill feat.

Water Yai

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.eeMEzXWaeh8Dw3fB]{Water Yai}

You're a water yai, an oni whose form is patterned after storm giants who typically love collecting things. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is storm giant (albeit a younger or smaller Large storm giant), and your language is Jotun. You gain the amphibious, giant, and water traits; like all creatures with the amphibious trait, you can breathe equally well on land or underwater. You can make a simple garment for yourself out of water, though it has no special effects. You gain a swim Speed of 15 feet.

Wind Yai

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.8XbBZtMQmN9BHsiR]{Wind Yai}

You're a wind yai, an oni whose form is patterned after a cloud giant and who typically seek to regain control of both their life and the world around them. Like many other wind yai, you might have a gloomy or fatalistic attitude, an edgelord aesthetic, or a love for music. Your size is Large, your associated humanoid is cloud giant, and your language is Jotun. You gain the air and giant traits. When you fall, you seize control of the air around you to avoid any danger. You don't take any damage from falling, regardless of the distance you fall.

Objective Standards.name: 客观标准 Objective Standards
Objective Standards.text:
摘自艾瑟里娅·尼克斯的档案
客观标准

在太多的文化中，“将他人物体化”的观念被反复提起。所幸，它总体上已消散在历史的迷雾之中。坦白说，这令人作呕。但这究竟是什么意思？物体化。这取决于我们所说的“物体”是什么意思。有些人将物体定义为我们周遭世界里任何有形的、可触的材料之物。但这并不具体，不是吗？可以说，这一定义适用于任何被物质本质所浸润的事物，包括活的事物、植物、动物，甚至你和我。“人”也一样，尽管这个特定定义本身就问题重重。但这并不是大多数人说“物体”时的意思，对吧？它通常指的是无生命的物体。“东西”也是一个具有同样古怪特性的词，理论上它包含一切，却又经常被用来与“人”相对照。把某人当作“物体”或“东西”，就是剥夺他们的能动性——要求他们扮演受事者而非施事者的角色。

这整套命名法引出了一个棘手的难题：该如何对待那些形态最容易让我们联想到物品与物体的人。智慧物品。直到最近，学者们在提及智慧物品的伙伴时仍在使用诸如“主人”之类的陈旧术语。令人厌恶。所幸，如今人们普遍接受：智慧物品与与其共事者是伙伴关系，是团队的一部分。这朝着正确方向迈出了一步，但也仅仅触及了智慧物品可能性的表面——其中智慧武器占了绝大多数。

你可能在某本魔法百科全书中读到的典型智慧物品，拥有自己的个性、智力、能力与能动性，但它们更像是智慧物品中的平民。它们能做什么就做什么，而那通常就是其魔法的极限。但有些智慧物品——尤其是智慧武器——还能做得更多。这总是源于不同寻常的境遇。在某片土地上，你或许会听到这样一个传说：一件智慧武器能够塑造自己的魅影身躯。在另一片土地上，则有智慧武器能自行打击的故事：当它不在行动时，便安放在发光的鞘上休息。还有遥远的低语，讲述一些智慧武器与其伙伴的羁绊在伙伴死亡之后、或在其心智死亡之后发生改变或仍得以延续。诸如此类以及其他各种故事，都各自包含一粒真实的砂砾。而智慧武器冒险者便由此诞生——它们能够成长、学习魔法、武艺等等技艺。所以，别再称他们为物体。

—— 艾瑟里娅·尼克斯，空间元素先驱，怪物倡议者

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Objective Standards

In far too many cultures, the idea of objectifying other people has been bandied about. Fortunately, it has generally been lost to the mists of history. It’s frankly revolting. But what does that mean exactly? Objectifying. It depends on what we mean by object. Some define an object as any material, tangible thing in the world around us. But that’s not very specific, is it? Arguably, that definition applies to anything infused with the essence of matter, including living things, plants, animals, and even you and me. "People" too, as much as that particular definition is fraught to begin with. But that’s not what most people mean when they say "object," is it? It typically means an inanimate object. "Thing" is another word that shares the same peculiarities, where it theoretically includes everything but is used in contrast to a "person" regularly. To consider someone an "object" or a "thing" is to rob them of their agency—to demand that they play the role of the patient rather than the agent.

This whole nomenclature leads to a difficult conundrum: what to do with the people whose forms remind us most of items and objects. Intelligent items. Until recently, scholars were still using archaic terms like "owner" with respect to an intelligent item’s partner. Repugnant. Fortunately, it is now commonly accepted that intelligent items and those who work with them are partners, part of a team. That’s a step in the right direction, but it only scratches the surface of what is possible for intelligent items, of which intelligent weapons represent the lion’s share.

While a typical intelligent item you might read about in a magical encyclopedia has its own personality, intellect, capabilities, and agency, they are something like the commoners of intelligent items. They can do what they can do, and that’s usually the limit of their magic. But some intelligent items, intelligent weapons, in particular, can do more. This always comes about through unusual circumstances. In one land, you might hear a tale of an intelligent weapon that can form its own phantom body. In another, of an intelligent weapon that strikes on its own, resting upon a glowing sheath when it isn’t in motion. And there are distant whispers of intelligent weapons whose bond to their partner changed or survived after the partner’s death or the death of their mind. These and all other sorts of stories each hold a grain of truth. And that is how intelligent weapon adventurers are born, able to grow and learn the techniques of magic, martial arts, and more. So don’t call them objects.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Intelligent Weapon Ancestry.name: 智能武器族裔 Intelligent Weapon Ancestry
Intelligent Weapon Ancestry.text:
In the most exciting stories of heroism, the heroes’ weapons stand out, with their names and powers passed down for generations. But sometimes, the weapon is the hero. Intelligent weapon characters possess intelligence and a spark of life, allowing them to break beyond the preconceptions of what an "item" can or should do and show the world that they are people like any other.

Intelligent weapon characters have origin stories that vary wildly from weapon to weapon. Some were always intelligent or created that way on purpose. Others were due to a curse or magical accident or even through unintentionally absorbing their creator’s or another creature’s mind. Or they became intelligent later on—possibly much later. Many cultures have specific legends describing this effect; for instance, oni believe that after 100 years, an object has a chance to gain a spirit and become sapient, and the existence of tsukumogami lends credence to this idea. Even beyond the first awakening to sapience, some intelligent weapons take quite some time before they progress from a normal intelligent weapon to an intelligent weapon character with increased agency and talents as a fighter, wizard, or other class.

Regardless of the timing and origin of an intelligent weapon’s awakening to sapience and agency as a character, many intelligent weapons experience a period of exploration and wanderlust after the process is complete. They often wish to learn about the world around them and experience its facets and nuances. However, some intelligent weapons avoid this tendency due to their awakening to intelligence due to incorporating an existing creature’s mind (often their creator), so they already have memories of the experiences from their old life.

Intelligent weapon characters sometimes have to be careful—not every society has laws nuanced enough to recognize their agency and dignity as sapient beings, and depending on their heritage, even societies that recognize their rights might ostracize them for manipulating the rights of others.

If you want to play a character whose true form is a magic weapon, you should play an intelligent weapon.

You Might...

  • Have a unique perspective on what it means to be an "object" or a "person."
  • Take the view that a whole (such as a wielder and weapon working in perfect sync) is greater than the sum of their parts.
  • Possess qualities related to your weapon’s form and function.

Others Probably...

  • Slip back into old habits without reminders, either thinking of you as an "item" or "object" or coming to unconsciously think of the body that wields you as the "real" you.
  • Expect that you possess marvelous magical properties in keeping with your intellect and agency.
  • Trust that you know your way around a fight.

Physical Description

Intelligent weapons look just like weapons of various types, though often surrounded by a visible glow or aura of powerful magic. Depending on their heritage, they might also have a projected avatar that wields them, a vacant body they’ve possessed, or a wielder who’s along for the ride. Each of these is visually distinctive, and the avatars and wielders can be from all sorts of ancestries. Ultimately, this means that despite their commonalities, intelligent weapons have among the greatest visual distinctiveness of all ancestries. Coming to life means that intelligent weapon characters are truly living creatures, complete with a mystical circulatory system that sends glowing magical power and life force throughout their weapon body, and this can lead to telltale signs if a perceptive and knowledgeable scholar investigates their appearance carefully.

Intelligent Weapon Adventurers

Intelligent weapon adventurers tend to choose classes that suit their outlook and their weapon. For instance, an intelligent rapier might become a rogue, swashbuckler, or investigator, while an intelligent pistol might become a gunslinger, and so on. Some intelligent weapons become inventors (choosing themself as an innovation) or thaumaturges (choosing themself as an implement). An intelligent weapon’s background varies depending on their interests, but they’re somewhat more likely to be a martial disciple, warrior, hunter, or other background that involves a weapon directly.

Society

Intelligent weapons don't awaken in communities with one another, except in extremely rare circumstances, such as an armory where a civilization mass produces intelligent weapons to use in war. Or in a workshop where a lone legendary blacksmith builds the family they never had, one intelligent weapon at a time. Regardless, they often find themselves drawn to each other, curious to share their experiences with others who have the same life experience, one so different from those living around them.

Arsenals

Although intelligent weapons can come from various backgrounds and experiences, they often end up with similar ideals and concepts of the world around them. Whether they complete their period of wanderlust, come with memories of an experienced individual, or go about learning in some other way, eventually a newly intelligent weapon develops a more mature perspective on their life. Sometimes, this is a personal philosophy, while other times, it's influenced by other, more experienced, intelligent weapons they met throughout their travels. The first intelligent weapon who codified these different philosophies decided to call them "arsenals," and the term stuck. Because of the unique experiences of each individual intelligent weapon, there are dozens, if not hundreds, of minor arsenals, but six stand out as especially popular: battleseekers, embodied, itemspeakers, ramblers, smiths, and upgraders.

Battleseekers

Weapons are made to fight, and so the greatest thrill and sense of purpose for an intelligent weapon must be in the heat of battle, clashing in mortal combat against another weapon and that weapon’s wielder to determine which weapon is stronger and whose skills are greater. Battle is when weapons can truly feel alive. Such is the credo of battleseekers, a popular arsenal among the more hotblooded and martially-minded intelligent weapons. Battleseekers tend to include some of the most individually powerful intelligent weapons of any arsenal, but they are often just that: individuals. A given battleseeker is usually much more interested in increasing their own personal skills and testing them out in a fight than they are in advancing the battleseeker arsenal’s philosophy in general or teaming up with other battleseekers to achieve a greater goal. In the same vein, when two battleseekers meet, it’s almost inevitable that it will come to a fight, at least until they can establish which of the two is stronger. These personality traits have kept the battleseeker arsenal from flexing their combat power against other arsenals, and indeed, most battleseekers see their fighting and increased prowess as more of an end than a means.

This individualism doesn’t mean that battleseekers can’t team up with other adventurers in order to grow stronger together, though they’re much less likely to team up with a rival intelligent weapon than with other ancestries. Adventuring companions of a battleseeker should expect regular duels and sparring matches, especially when the battleseeker or a companion gains an exciting new special move or significant increase in combat ability. This helps the battleseeker continue to hone their strength and gauge their allies’ progress in relation to their own. If their allies are outpacing them, the battleseeker must contemplate deeply and determine the cause in order to improve their growth, and if the battleseeker is the one advancing fastest, they might need to find a way to pull their companions up so they don’t stop being useful sparring partners. While others sometimes mistake battleseekers as thoughtless, that’s hardly the case; they simply don’t care to exercise their intellect toward the same topics as others. When it comes to analyzing fighting styles and combat growth or any other topic that strikes their interest, they can demonstrate exceptional insight.

Embodied

Embodied are intelligent weapon characters who come to associate their concept of self so deeply with the body of their wielder (whether it’s a projected avatar or another creature’s body) that they consider their wielder to be a fundamental part of their identity. For this reason, embodied never have the animated weapon heritage, and they are much less likely to have the symbiotic weapon heritage than they are to have the possessing or projecting weapon heritages. Out of all the arsenals, embodied are the intelligent weapons that can most easily blend into humanoid societies, introducing themselves as their wielder to the extent that sometimes they might become famous without anyone ever realizing they’re an intelligent weapon.

Of course, in some cases, there’s a compelling reason to become an embodied: intelligent weapons possessing the intelligence of their creator or another creature, through accident or intent, often still identify as the original creature in question and wish to have a body to continue this prior identity. This is doubly true if they’re projecting an idealized version of their original body or possessing their vegetative original body. In fact, if they were already famous before becoming an intelligent weapon, those around them might not even realize that something happened to them as they continue to live out their former life alongside this "amazing new weapon." Even if they don’t want to continue their old life, an embodied possessing another creature’s intelligence might still miss having a physical body.

Embodied tend to be fairly easy to get along with, but they still sometimes quarrel with itemspeakers, as the latter arsenal feels that abandoning what makes intelligent weapons special and unique and consigning other items to mistreatment in order to "blend in" with humanoid society. For their part, most embodied don’t have the same issues with itemspeakers as the more quarrelsome itemspeakers do with them, though embodied whose mind originally came from another ancestry might take offense at the idea that they can’t or shouldn’t identify as a member of their original ancestry.

Improvised Intelligent Weapons?

Improvised weapons can’t be magic items, and thus, by the rules, your intelligent weapon can’t be an improvised weapon. However, if you’d like to play an intelligent tankard of ale, spoon, or similar object, you could consider working with the GM to find stats for a similar weapon and make your character a non-improvised tankard of ale bespoke weapon, perhaps based on the statistics of a club. As a true weapon, not an improvised weapon, you would use the normal rules for manufactured weapons and would not be eligible for the weapon improvisor archetype and similar mechanics.

Itemspeakers

Intelligent weapons are some of the few items with a voice that other creatures can hear and understand, but that doesn’t mean other "mundane" items don’t have their thoughts and spirits growing within them. Psychometry and object reading, along with creatures such as tsukumogami or poppets wished to life by a child’s love, prove that ordinary objects can become imbued with thoughts, feelings, and even spirits. And just because these items are earlier in the process of developing their inner spirit doesn’t mean it’s right to disrespect and abuse them.

To an itemspeaker, maliciously ripping off the arms of a cherished doll is akin to tearing off the limbs of a grasshopper. That’s not to say that wear and breakage during regular use are a problem; those items are proud of fulfilling their role, and breaking over the long process of their intended use is a respectable fate, especially for those willing to help repair and reuse them rather than toss them aside.

Intelligent weapons of the itemspeaker arsenal feel that their role as weapons that live, think, and speak in the traditional sense is more easily respected and understood by humanoids and other similar creatures, bestows them with a responsibility to speak for all the other items of the world who don’t have the same kind of voice. To let people know what their items are feeling—not only will that help the humanoid learn what their treatment of the item could mean if and when the item develops a stronger spirit of its own and comes to life, it’s simply the right thing to do, even if the item never progresses beyond faint psychic impressions experienced via psychometry and object reading. While their philosophy makes itemspeakers extremely empathetic and respectful of the agency of the oppressed, it can sometimes lead them to dogmatic views and negative reactions when they come into conflict with those who mock and deride their perspective. Occasionally, a humanoid feeling contrary or annoyed that someone would dare preach this strange philosophy to them begins to wantonly destroy inanimate objects while the itemspeaker watches, just to prove a point, and this can quickly escalate into a violent conflict if the itemspeaker takes the bait.

Animated Weapon Limitations

While the ability to float allows you to avoid quite a few hindrances on the battlefield, the lack of a free hand is a major limitation for animated weapons that helps balance out that powerful benefit. As a result, however, animated weapons are much better off choosing to be a weapon that uses two hands (or "1+" hands, like a bow), and they have a lot of trouble as thaumaturges, alchemists, or other classes heavily reliant on having a free hand.

Ramblers

Not every intelligent weapon "grows out" of the wanderlust and desire to experience everything the world has to offer. Intelligent weapons of the rambler arsenal choose to continue their adventures and exploration for the long haul, or even forever, though some eventually transition to another arsenal or develop a hybrid philosophy with another arsenal. Ramblers chose their name because of their desire to aimlessly ramble through the world and see all it has to offer rather than simply go where they must to complete tasks or missions. However, they also love to tell lengthy stories of their travels, sometimes to an excessive degree, and so occasionally other arsenals use the other meaning of "ramble" to tease them for that a bit. Two ramblers who meet on the road often spend days, if not longer, sharing all the stories of their respective adventures as they sit rapt at the edge of their scabbard through every minute.

Along with battleseekers, ramblers are one of the most common arsenals to become adventurers, though they chafe in adventuring parties who are tied to one location or overly focused on a single mission or goal. Exploring what’s beyond the unknown hill in a new land is much more interesting to a rambler than heading to the lich’s castle to put down an undead uprising, even if they each required an equal amount of travel.

When left to their own devices, ramblers often explore or examine everything around them. While others can get the wrong idea and view ramblers as being absent-minded, in truth, their attention to these details can lead to them noticing secrets or clues that others might miss, even if they weren’t listening to the conversation everyone else was having.

Key Terms

Activation: Many of the feats in the intelligent weapon ancestry have the "activation" trait. A feat with the activation trait either provides a new activation or, if the feat is an action, the feat is an activation. These feats are available in inanimate mode, to a projecting weapon without a projection, or to an item intelligent weapon not using the full ancestry, background, and class rules (see @UUID[.7thpmGycgWhx5P5U] for more information on playing an item intelligent weapon); if an activation feat in inanimate mode, without a projection, or as an item, grants an action you can’t use (such as a Strike or Stride), you don’t take that action, and the GM might determine the entire spell has no effect if the action was essential or a balancing factor. Typically, such a situation occurs when an activation feat allows you to choose a spell, and you choose a spell that grants an action you can’t use, so be careful when choosing such a spell. When you’re in inanimate mode with another wielder, or if you’re playing as an item, your wielder can also use your activation feats with your permission; if they do so, they can take actions like Strike or Stride.

Smiths

Despite their differences, the intelligent weapons of the world are part of a community, a broader family. And in order to grow this family, the most important work is smithing, specifically learning the unique magical crafting skills necessary to forge more intelligent weapons. The smith arsenal wholeheartedly believes this ideal, and so they strive to increase their understanding of weapons, especially intelligent weapons, in order to grow their family and bring more intelligent weapons into the world. Smiths work constantly and continually to improve their skills and to create ever-greater masterpieces, which they regard as their beloved children.

Artistry and self-expression in their creative work are just as important to many smiths as artisanry and functionality. These smiths believe that the personality and soul of an intelligent weapon built with expressive artistic vision can soar to greater heights than those created formulaically and purely with utility and function in mind. Many smiths similarly refuse to create near-identical weapons twice, instead preferring to forge a wide array of different weapons, with each unique and distinctive from the rest in both magical and physical properties.

Some smiths get so obsessed with their own work that they keep to their own devices, but for most others, the aspiration of belonging and family that guide their forging process makes them friendly, or at least harmless. But those few malevolent smiths are a blight to the world, forging ever-increasing numbers of malicious, cursed intelligent weapons to expand their cruel family and slaughter or dominate other ancestries.

Intelligent Weapon Homes

Depending on their heritage, some intelligent weapons enjoy living in spaces similar to humanoids, but others have eclectic tastes, preferring to rest in a nice comfortable scabbard (which feels to them like a blanket for a humanoid) or display room, with a training ground or dueling ring as another must.

Upgraders

Upgraders agree with battleseekers that the pursuit of perfection is worthwhile, but they see the other arsenal as hopelessly naive. Battleseekers are caught up in the idea that the battle is the be-all-end-all, but truly, battles are simply one tool in the toolkit of improving oneself and one of the bluntest, least elegant, and most wasteful tools of the bunch. To an upgrader, the path of self-perfection involves a combination of all possible tools and techniques, focusing especially on ways of becoming stronger that are uniquely available to intelligent weapons. After all, any other ancestry out there can clash and clash with each other and hope to become stronger if they’re lucky and persistent, but only intelligent weapons can calmly, methodically upgrade themselves as weapons, spending time and resources to steadily grow stronger without taking unnecessary risks or employing inefficient strategies.

Upgraders don’t all agree on what it means to perfect oneself and "upgrade." While the typical upgrader includes a strong component of magical power in their definition, many also incorporate other factors such as aesthetics, composition, structural integrity, and distinctiveness. Some unusual upgraders couldn’t care less about their capacity as a magic weapon and care only about an aesthetic and distinctive appearance, even if such a form makes them less effective when used as a weapon. Other upgraders consider this view to be quite shallow, akin to someone who cares only for the outward physical appearance of others rather than the person’s inner self.

Upgraders are always looking for new and unique ways to improve themselves and their capacities, and thus, few upgraders can resist rumors of lost magical weapon techniques, runes, monster part crafting, or other similar processes. In that regard, they can find themselves either teamed up with or in open rivalry against a smith who is after the same techniques to use for their children rather than themself.

Alignment and Religion

Intelligent weapons have alignments as varied as any other creature, depending on their circumstances. Like many other ancestries, they tend slightly toward more neutral alignments in a vacuum, but intelligent weapons might spark intellect from an aligned creature or be forged as a holy blade or cursed weapon, granting them an especially extreme alignment, so these circumstances typically even out the odds of any alignment.

Intelligent weapons aren’t especially likely to be religious, other than those deriving their sapience from a religious creature or forged as a religious weapon, but those who do deeply venerate a deity have a slight preference toward deities presiding over battle or crafting, such as Lilin the Mother, goddess of artistry and creation, or Zatera, goddess of war. Depending on their arsenal and personal views, intelligent weapons might also worship a wide variety of other deities.

Names

Intelligent weapons are often named by their creator with a name sounding more descriptive or fanciful, like a non-intelligent weapon’s name, but some creators give a weapon a name from their own ancestry or an especially unusual name. Additionally, some intelligent weapons reject their creator’s name and create their own brand new name based on their own identity. Those intelligent weapons who gained the intellect of another creature might take the name of that creature as their own; in fact, depending on their heritage, such an intelligent weapon might be able to pick their life back up right where they left off, with others assuming they found a powerful new weapon instead of realizing they became the weapon.

As a result of all these competing trends, there’s no unifying theme to intelligent weapon names, and they can include names as disparate as a single letter or serial number and a perfectly ordinary name from another ancestry. The sample names below are listed for convenience and are meant to show the wide variety of possibilities rather than to be indicative of the full breadth of variety.

Sample Names

&, Anglachel, Gurthang, Kring, Kullervo, Muramasa, N, Need, Nightblood, Orna, Pyra, Soul, Stormbringer, Survivor, Teacher, Tremble, Umbra, Zangetsu.

Intelligent Weapon Convocations

Intelligent weapons usually spend much more time among other creatures of various different ancestries than they do among other intelligent weapons, and living intelligent weapons are even rarer still. Even so, occasionally, a group of living intelligent weapons will hold a convocation, a grand gathering of every living intelligent weapon they know (and sometimes a few item intelligent weapons with a trusted partner who’s willing to bring them to the convocation, but only if the organizers know that weapon and partner well). The name "convocation" might sound weighty, but these affairs tend to be convivial and relaxed, providing living intelligent weapons a place where they can talk about their unique challenges with others who understand.

Intelligent Weapon Heritages

Intelligent weapon characters manifest their agency in different ways, each represented by a different heritage. These heritages have a major impact on how your character interacts with the world and will help determine your character’s effective size (the weapon part of your character is always Tiny, but most heritages are usually effectively Medium or Small when active as a character). Choose one of the following intelligent weapon heritages at 1st level.

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.D7248cAhlmtwnFPp]{Animated Weapon}

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.Gf0Ko7wt5im5nHIl]{Possessing Weapon}

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.qO68wDyiZRHd8qzl]{Projecting Weapon}

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-heritages.Item.LKXWzeNfg5XfxUrD]{Symbiotic Weapon}

If you choose a versatile heritage instead, decide whether or not you have a wielder. If you don’t, use the animated weapon heritage’s rules except that you don’t gain the ability to float (you slide along the ground), and if you do, use the rules for possessing, projecting, or symbiotic weapon without their special benefit (no benefit from the chosen ancestry if you’re possessing a body, no ability to turn on and off the projection if you’re projecting a body, and no extra skills or ability to gain ancestry benefits briefly for an action if you’re symbiotic with a wielder). Essentially, you gain the trappings of the chosen heritage without the mechanical benefit, plus all the benefits of your versatile heritage.

Whether you’re a possessing, projecting, or symbiotic weapon, you have a wielder of some kind, which you, the player, control. You, the intelligent weapon, and the wielder are essentially a single character, with the Hit Points indicated by your Constitution, class, level, and other features "shared" between both the weapon and the wielder. You and your wielder can’t be targeted separately, you and your wielder aren’t affected twice in an area effect, and so on. Whatever affects you as a weapon also affects the wielder, and your wielder wears any worn items, carries equipment, and so on, granting you the benefit, as you and the wielder are effectively a single creature. However, the way your wielder holds onto the equipment can be inconvenient if you’re a possessing or symbiotic weapon separated from your wielder and left without your items, as well as if you’re a projecting weapon and you unmanifest your avatar, leaving all the equipment and items your avatar was wearing and carrying on the ground in your space.

In rare instances, a symbiotic weapon might become forcibly separated from their wielder, which makes things slightly more complicated than the rules in the above paragraph; the rules of that heritage indicate how to handle this situation. Possessing and projecting weapons, however, are necessary for their wielder to move (for possessing weapons) or exist (for projecting weapons).

Ancestry Feats

The following feats are available to intelligent weapon characters. If an intelligent weapon feat mentions making a Strike "with yourself," and so on, it means when using yourself as a weapon; if you gain unarmed attacks that emerge from your weapon body, such as if you're an animated weapon using a morph effect to grow jaws, your unarmed attacks don't count as attacking "with yourself."

17th Level

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-year-of-monsters-pf2e.yom-feats.Item.bZ6KpbRIqzLgbm6e]{Animated Hardness}
